# SpongeJIT Lua SSA Compiler ASDL Rewrite 
Design proper ASDL vocabulary for a complete rewrite of SpongeJIT Lua SSA compiler. Guides: COMPILER_PATTERN.md and PVM_GUIDE.md. Principle: SSA consumes Lua opcode semantics into least/fastest canonical representation; residency and semantic mechanics solved cleanly in separate layers.
**Workflow ID**: wf-sponjit-ssa-asdl-rewrite
**Started**: 2026-06-01 11:49:32
---

# SponJIT Foundry SSA — Atom Vocabulary Design

**Status:** current design/implementation notes for the maintained native-stencil pipeline. The foundry emits SSA-consumed normal forms and lowers supported classes to executable x86-64 native stencil bytes plus explicit relocation metadata.

---

## One-sentence purpose

The SponJIT foundry enumerates local atom candidates, specializes them with explicit facts through semantic SSA, lowers useful candidates to Stencil IR and native stencil descriptors/bytes, and builds the vocabulary that Tier 2 later fuses over long hot spans.

---

## Current pipeline

The maintained pipeline is:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA consumes opcode semantics and facts
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ lower one representative per supported class to native x86-64 stencil bytes
→ ABI-lowered descriptor metadata + byte bank
```

Equivalence class key for grammar/foundry mode:

```text
canonical_stencil_hash
```

The source opcode sequence and fact-transfer contract are **not** part of the normal-form key. Many opcode/fact candidates may collapse to one final form after SSA. Their source patterns are retained as aliases for selection; the stencil body/descriptor is emitted once.

## Loop boundary stance

`FORPREP`/`FORLOOP` and `TFOR*` are not scalar opcodes for the current fact-axis pass. They are structural loop-region boundaries. Until SpongeJIT has paired loop-region lowering with explicit loop edges, full loop-window slotmaps, topology bindings, and edge-sensitive fact transfer, the foundry must not advertise i64 loop fact bundles. Numeric and generic loops therefore lower only to exact VM boundary handoffs, not partially specialized stencils.

Important modules:

```text
spongejit/src/grammar_enum.lua       -- grammar / sequence enumeration
spongejit/src/enumerate.lua          -- corpus/window enumeration
spongejit/src/loop_regions.lua       -- paired numeric/generic loop topology records
spongejit/src/ssa_lift.lua           -- opcode/facts -> semantic SSA
spongejit/src/ssa_ir.lua             -- SSA graph/value/effect model
spongejit/src/ssa_opt.lua            -- local semantic cleanup
spongejit/src/ssa.lua                -- public compile facade
spongejit/src/ssa_atoms.lua          -- reopen serialized node specs
spongejit/src/stencil_ir.lua         -- canonical hole-parametric shape
spongejit/src/ssa_to_stencil.lua     -- SSA -> Stencil IR
spongejit/src/stencil_normalize.lua  -- canonical key/hash/form
spongejit/src/stencil_lower.lua       -- Stencil IR -> native stencil descriptor
spongejit/src/stencil_native_x64.lua  -- first-surface x86-64 byte emitter
spongejit/src/stencil_desc.lua        -- stencil schema, validation, ABI lowering
spongejit/src/worker_compile.lua     -- worker SQLite metadata artifacts
spongejit/src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua   -- global normal-form dedupe + unique lowering
```

Chunked metadata-bank build:

```sh
cd experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit
make stencils        # CHUNKS=128, WORKERS=$(nproc) by default
make bank             # libsponbank.so with native bytes + descriptors from stencil_bank.sqlite
```

`CHUNKS` and `WORKERS` are separate controls. More chunks reduce per-worker shard size; more workers increase parallelism.

SQLite outputs:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

Bank outputs:

```text
build/sponbank/libsponbank.so
build/sponbank/sponbank_build_manifest.json
```

The generated shared library contains native stencil byte bodies for supported first-surface classes, plus endpoint, projection, slotmap, data-reloc, control-reloc, and selector metadata. It is still not a full runtime: the online fusion planner and copy/link/patch materializer remain separate work.

---

## Foundry vs Tier 2

The foundry builds local vocabulary. Tier 2 builds long-range fusions.

```text
Foundry:
  generate accepted native atoms and variants

Tier 2 online fusion planner:
  compose many atoms into one FusionPlan
```

A local atom may come from any foundry layer: L0, L1, L2, L3, or later. Tier 2 does not care which layer produced it; it sees a stream of atom candidates with explicit contracts.

---

## Atom vocabulary target

The current saturation target is local:

```text
atom arity <= 4
~32 fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is enough material to test the Tier 2 thesis because long traces are not precomputed as single giant atoms. Tier 2 composes many local atoms:

```text
4000 atom hot span
  -> FusionPlan over 4000 atom instances
  -> one future LinkedImage
```

The arity/fact-combo cap shapes the vocabulary. It does not cap the Tier 2 optimization range.

---

## Layer growth model

The foundry basis grows by accepting valid native atoms from local compositions:

```text
L0 = raw opcode atoms
for layer k:
  basis = union(L0..Lk)
  candidates = local tuples over basis, arity <= 4
  for candidate in candidates:
    for fact_env in fact combinations:
      ssa = specialize_with_facts(candidate, fact_env)
      stencil = lower_to_stencil(ssa)
      stencil = lower_to_native_stencil(stencil)
      accept iff semantic SSA, normal form validation, and native lowering all pass
  L{k+1} = unique accepted native normal forms from this layer
```

Rejected candidates are measurements, not runtime artifacts.

---

## Semantic SSA role

Semantic SSA is the fact-consuming layer.

It is responsible for:

- preserving opcode semantics;
- making fact consequences explicit;
- modeling effects, memory tokens, guards, exits, dependencies, and value residencies;
- exposing source PC and exit metadata;
- producing a graph suitable for Stencil IR lowering.

It is not a runtime IR. The future runtime must not build or optimize SSA.

---

## Stencil IR role

Stencil IR is the canonical materialization shape between semantic SSA and stencil descriptors.

It represents:

- stencil ops;
- values;
- data holes;
- slotmaps;
- facts;
- exits;
- source ops;
- effect/projection-relevant metadata.

Important invariant:

```text
exit/fail are not data holes
```

Exits are endpoint/projection/control concepts.

---

## Stencil lowering role

`stencil_lower.lua` lowers Stencil IR into native stencil descriptors and invokes the first-surface x86-64 byte emitter.

Current first surface:

```text
i64 slot loads/stores
i64 guards
unbox/box-relevant i64 operations
simple constants/immediates
simple exact boundary exits
```

Supported simple data roles:

```text
slot
slot_store
imm
const
bool
```

Payload-heavy roles require explicit payload leases and dependency invalidation before executable use.

---

## Output atom contract

An atom must eventually expose enough structure for Tier 2 fusion:

```text

# SponJIT Architecture — Native Stencil Vocabulary + Online Fusion

**Status:** current design overview. The maintained implementation now produces executable native-stencil byte banks for the supported first surface. No copy/link/patch runtime is wired into the Lalin VM yet.

---

## One-sentence architecture

SponJIT builds a vocabulary of preverified native-stencil atoms offline, then Tier 2 performs long-range online fusion over those atoms to produce a `FusionPlan` that a mechanical copy/link/patch materializer can turn into one executable image.

```text
foundry/offline:
  bytecode/facts -> SSA -> Stencil IR -> abstract/native stencil atoms

Tier 2 online:
  hot atom span + facts -> FusionPlan

materializer/next:
  FusionPlan + NativeStencilBank -> LinkedImage
```

---

## Current implementation reality

The live maintained pipeline is now a native stencil-bank build:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA consumes opcode semantics and facts
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ native x86-64 byte stencil for one representative
→ ABI-lowered descriptor metadata + byte bank
```

Key modules:

```text
spongejit/src/ssa_ir.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_lift.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_opt.lua
spongejit/src/ssa.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_atoms.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_ir.lua
spongejit/src/ssa_to_stencil.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_normalize.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_lower.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_desc.lua
spongejit/src/stencil_projection.lua
spongejit/src/worker_compile.lua
spongejit/src/dedupe_normal_forms.lua
```

Current chunked outputs from `spongejit/build_stencils.sh` / `make stencils`:

```text
grammar_chunk_N.json
grammar_worker_N.sqlite
stencil_bank.sqlite  -- unique normal forms, lowered stencils, pattern aliases
```

`make bank` consumes `stencil_bank.sqlite` and builds `build/sponbank/libsponbank.so`, including native byte bodies for supported stencils plus descriptor/selector arrays.

There is currently no maintained copy/link/patch runtime, native stencil linker, or Lalin VM integration.

---

## Vocabulary

### Atom

An **atom** is a precomputed stencil template in the bank vocabulary. It may come from any foundry layer: L0, L1, L2, L3, or later. The layer does not matter to Tier 2 once the atom is in the vocabulary.

### Stencil

A **native stencil** is a relocatable native-code region with explicit endpoints, data relocations, control relocations, fact transfer, clobbers, and projection metadata.

An **abstract stencil** is a non-executable descriptor for future analysis surfaces. Unsupported opcode/fact cases now reject before materialization rather than becoming executable fallback stubs.

### FusionPlan

A `FusionPlan` is the Tier 2 product: a long-range composition of atom instances plus explicit success edges, exit edges, fact flow, projection obligations, seam decisions, and materialization choices.

### LinkedImage

A `LinkedImage` is the runtime product: one executable image created by copying stencil bytes, patching data/control relocations, installing projection stubs, and publishing it atomically. The maintained tree now has a standalone Lua materializer smoke path for straight-line images; production publishing/VM integration is still future work.

---

## Phase tree

| Phase | Product | Current? |
|---|---|---|
| Foundry enumeration | opcode/fact atom candidates | yes |
| Semantic SSA | fact-specialized semantic graph | yes |
| Stencil IR | canonical hole-parametric shape | yes |
| Native stencil emission | executable stencil bytes + real relocs | yes, first surface |
| Tier 2 online fusion planner | `FusionPlan` over atoms | design / next work |
| Standalone copy/link/patch materializer | executable straight-line image | yes, first surface |
| Runtime image publisher / VM materializer | `LinkedImage` | future |
| Lalin VM integration | active image execution from `src/vm_loop.lua` | gated off |

---

## Foundry role

The foundry creates the atom vocabulary. It may do expensive work offline:

- enumerate local atom tuples;
- apply fact combinations;
- build semantic SSA;
- lower to Stencil IR;
- validate stencil descriptors;
- dedupe canonical normal forms after SSA consumes semantics;
- retain accepted native normal forms for later-layer composition.

The working saturation target is local:

```text
atom arity <= 4
~32 fact combinations per atom shape
```

This is not a Tier 2 trace limit. It is a vocabulary target.

---

## Tier 2 role

Tier 2 does long-range fusion over atom streams.

```text
4000 atoms
  -> online fusion planner
  -> one FusionPlan
  -> one LinkedImage after materialization
```

Tier 2 may optimize by choosing among prebuilt atoms, variants, endpoints, bridges, and projection stubs:

- direct atom-to-atom continuations;
- hot fallthrough layout;
- bridge insertion or omission;
- store/load seam removal;
- box/unbox seam removal;
- redundant guard removal;
- projection-stub sharing;
- cold exit outlining;
- dependency-aware variant choice.

Tier 2 must not synthesize arbitrary native instructions. It optimizes the stencil graph, not machine code.

---

## Why granular atoms are enough

Small atoms give the fusion planner flexibility. If the bank has atom variants with precise contracts, a long fused image can be trace-like without a runtime code generator.

The required contracts are:

- input/output locations;
- clobbers;
- fact transfer;
- data relocations;
- control relocations;
- projection recipes;
- dependency obligations;
- endpoint compatibility.

If atoms only expose frame-synced boundaries, Tier 2 mainly removes dispatch/linking overhead. If atoms expose register/value-flow endpoints and reconstructing projections, Tier 2 can remove frame seams and behave much more like a trace optimizer.

---

## Runtime role

The future runtime is deliberately simple compared with a tracing compiler:

```text
observe stable hot span
  -> invoke online fusion planner over bank atoms
  -> materialize chosen FusionPlan
  -> publish active image
  -> on exit, project state and fall back/demote/replan
```

The runtime does not run SSA, invent new instructions, infer projection recipes, or patch live images in place.

---

## Floor and active image

When executable SponJIT exists, each enabled region has:

```text
floor   always-correct native baseline image
active  current selected image; may equal floor
```

The floor requires exact semantic native coverage for the enabled opcode subset, and missing coverage is reported explicitly. Until such a floor exists, SponJIT remains a foundry/planner prototype and the Lalin VM uses its normal interpreter path.

---

## Relation to Lalin VM

The Lalin interpreter VM in `src/` is not JIT-integrated today. `src/contract.lua` keeps:

```lua
sponjit_allowed = false
```

SponJIT must not become an execution engine for `src/vm_loop.lua` until VM gates around bytecode validation, frame/cache semantics, native ABI, allocation, errors, and coroutine/yield behavior are satisfied.

---

## Canonical documents

| Document | Role |
|---|---|
| `SPONJIT_FOUNDRY_SSA.md` | Foundry/SSA atom vocabulary design. |

# SponJIT Runtime Design — Native Stencil Image Materialization

**Type:** future runtime/materializer specification  
**Methodology:** explicit programming  
**Status:** runtime/materializer design; native byte bank exists for the first surface, copy/link/patch runtime not implemented  
**Depends on:** `SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` and `SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md`

---

## 0. Current reality

The maintained SponJIT implementation currently has a **native byte stencil bank** for the supported first surface:

```text
opcode sequence + fact bundle
→ semantic SSA
→ canonical Stencil IR normal form
→ global normal-form equivalence class
→ native x86-64 stencil bytes for one representative
→ grammar_worker_N.sqlite → stencil_bank.sqlite → libsponbank.so
```

There is currently no maintained copy/link/patch SponJIT runtime:

- no C-function tile bank;
- no maintained PUC runtime integration;
- no production native stencil linker/runtime publisher;
- only a standalone Lua copy/link/patch smoke materializer for straight-line first-surface images;
- no Lalin VM integration.

The Lalin interpreter VM gate remains closed:

```lua
sponjit_allowed = false
```

This document is therefore not a description of a working runtime. It is the runtime design that consumes the proof-bearing native stencil bank once the online fusion planner and materializer are implemented.

---

## 1. One-sentence purpose

The SponJIT runtime observes hot spans, invokes an online fusion planner over preverified native stencil templates, materializes the chosen fusion plan by copying bytes and patching data/control relocations, publishes that image atomically, and falls back through projection-safe exits without running arbitrary SSA optimization, register allocation, or code generation at runtime.

That sentence is the boundary:

```text
runtime may:       observe, plan fusion over bank stencils, select, copy, link, patch, publish, demote, log
runtime may not:   compile arbitrary code, synthesize instructions, infer hidden semantics, execute abstract stencils
```

---

## 2. Relationship to the other SponJIT specs

### 2.1 Online fusion planner spec

`SPONJIT_TIER2_PLANNER_SPEC.md` defines the planner used in two binding-time modes.

Planner input mode:

```text
native stencil bank + observed facts + site memory
→ FusionPlan / FloorOnly / RejectionReport / Deferral
```

The runtime does not run a general compiler. It may run the online fusion planner over prebuilt native stencil templates. That planner can choose variants, bridges, endpoints, seams, and layout policies from the bank vocabulary; the materializer then realizes the chosen plan mechanically.

### 2.2 Native stencil ABI spec

`SPONJIT_COPY_LINK_PATCH.md` defines the future native stencil ABI:

```text
native bytes + endpoint metadata + data relocs + control relocs + projection recipes
```

This runtime design is the consumer of that ABI.

### 2.3 Runtime design boundary

This document starts at the first point where the system has a conforming native stencil bank:

```text
NativeStencilBank
  -> online fusion planning
  -> FusionPlan
  -> MaterializationPlan
  -> LinkedImage
  -> active execution
```

If a selected artifact is abstract rather than native, runtime materialization must return:

```text
Deferred(NeedsNativeStencils)
```

---

## 3. Retired terminology and replacement terminology

The old documents used `TileTemplate`, C stencils, and C-function-shaped tiles. That terminology is retired for maintained SponJIT runtime design.

Use these terms instead:

| Old term | New term | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| TileTemplate | NativeStencilTemplate | Relocatable native code region with explicit endpoints. |
| tile image | LinkedImage | Executable region image built from stencil instances. |
| tile exit | StencilExit | Non-success endpoint reached from native stencil code. |
| tile-site memory | StencilSiteMemory | Per-span selector memory for exits, penalties, cooldowns. |
| C stencil | non-conforming artifact | C functions are not linkable native stencils. |

`L0` remains as a layer concept, but it means exact semantic native stencils for the implemented floor subset. Missing opcode coverage is reported explicitly; it is not hidden behind fallback stubs.

---

## 4. Runtime thesis

SponJIT is not a tracing JIT in the LuaJIT sense: it does not record IR and emit arbitrary native code at runtime. Tier 2 can still behave like a long-range trace optimizer by optimizing a hot span as a **stencil graph** over prebuilt native stencils.

```text
LuaJIT-like system:
  runtime observes -> records -> optimizes IR -> emits native code -> caches traces

SponJIT:
  foundry builds a verified native stencil vocabulary offline
  runtime observes a hot span -> online fusion planner chooses a legal optimized stencil graph
  materializer copy/link/patches that plan -> publishes image
```

The bank is immutable. The runtime builds images from the bank. The runtime never creates a new code shape that was not already present as a native stencil template.

The central bet is:

```text
copy/link/patch is cheap enough that runtime adaptation can be
stencil-graph fusion, not live machine-code compilation.
```

### 4.1 Atom arity is not Tier 2 trace length

The foundry may precompute native stencil templates as atoms from any bank layer: L1, L2, L3, or later layers. An atom may itself represent a fused phrase over lower-layer atoms. That does **not** cap Tier 2 image length.

Tier 2 does long-range fusion over those atoms, whatever layer they come from:

```text
4000-atom hot span
  -> FusionPlan(atom_instance_1, atom_instance_2, ..., atom_instance_N)
  -> one LinkedImage
  -> enter once, run direct continuations, exit through projection stubs
```

The layer/arity of one atom describes the size of an individual template. Tier 2 optimization range is the length of the atom trace it chooses to fuse.

### 4.2 Initial bank saturation assumption

The current design target is a granular atom vocabulary: saturate atom generation to local arity 4 and keep roughly 32 fact combinations per atom shape after canonicalization and dedupe.

This is a local vocabulary assumption, not a Tier 2 trace limit:

```text
arity <= 4 per atom
~32 fact combos per atom shape
many atoms per FusionPlan
one linked image per hot span
```

That vocabulary is only valuable if atom descriptors expose value-flow structure: endpoint locations, fact transfer, clobbers, projection recipes, bridge compatibility, and variants that avoid forced frame synchronization. Without those contracts, Tier 2 gets mostly direct-linking wins. With them, Tier 2 can behave like a trace optimizer assembled from prebuilt atoms.

---

## 5. Runtime phase tree

The runtime phase tree is explicit:

| Phase | Input | Product | May fail as |
|---|---|---|---|
| Observation | active/floor execution events | `RuntimeObservation` | never; can be empty |
| Signature canonicalization | observations + fact schema | `ObservedSignature` | `UnsupportedObservation` |
| Candidate enumeration | bank + signature + site memory | `CandidateSet` | `NoCandidate`, then floor candidates |
| Online fusion planning | candidates + facts + endpoints + projections | `FusionPlan` / `StencilCover` | `FloorOnly`, `Deferred`, `Rejected` |
| Materialization planning | fusion plan + bank metadata | `MaterializationPlan` | `Deferred`, `Rejected` |
| Image building | materialization plan | writable `ImageBuild` | allocation/reloc failure |
| Patching | image build + runtime values | patched writable image | patch failure |
| Permission install | patched image | executable image | platform failure |
| Publication | executable image | active pointer update | never publishes partial image |
| Exit handling | stencil exit | projected fallback resume | projection failure is fatal/debug-only bug |
| Invalidation | dependency epoch advance | active demotion to floor | never continues stale image |

The runtime never jumps from fusion planning directly to execution. Every executable image passes through materialization and publication.

---

## 6. Runtime data tree

The runtime data tree is the semantic shape. C structs may lower these types, but the wire layout is not the design root.

SponRuntime = {

  policy: RuntimePolicy,
}
```

```text

  pc_end: SourcePc,

  floor: LinkedImage,

-- ssa_ir.lua -- typed SSA graph with explicit memory/effect/exit objects.

local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
    local out = {}
    for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
    return out
end

local function sorted_keys(t)
    local out = {}
    for k in pairs(t or {}) do out[#out + 1] = k end
    table.sort(out)
    return out
end

local VALUE_TYPES = {
    TValue = true, I64 = true, F64 = true, Bool = true,
    PtrTable = true, PtrClosure = true, Shape = true,
    FieldAddr = true, Void = true, Unknown = true,
}

local EFFECTS = {
    none = true, guard = true, frame_read = true, frame_write = true,
    heap_read = true, heap_write = true, gc_barrier = true, call = true,
    branch = true, return_ = true,
}

local CODEGEN_OP = {
    FrameLoad = "load_slot",
    FrameStore = "store_slot",
    LoadConst = "load_const",
    ConstI64 = "const_i64",
    ConstF64 = "const_f64",
    ConstNil = "const_nil",
    ConstBool = "const_bool",
    Move = "move_value",
    GuardTypeI64 = "guard_i64",
    GuardNumber = "guard_number",
    GuardNonZeroI64 = "guard_nonzero_i64",
    GuardTable = "guard_table",
    GuardShape = "guard_shape",
    GuardMetatableAbsent = "guard_metatable_absent",
    GuardCallTarget = "guard_call_target",
    GuardArrayHit = "guard_array_hit",
    GuardBounds = "guard_bounds",
    UnboxI64 = "unbox_i64",
    ToF64 = "to_f64",
    PowF64 = "pow_f64",
    BoxI64 = "box_i64",
    UpvalueLoad = "upvalue_load",
    UpvalueStore = "upvalue_store",
    AddI64 = "add_i64",
    SubI64 = "sub_i64",
    MulI64 = "mul_i64",
    DivI64ToF64 = "div_i64_to_f64",
    I64BinOp = "i64_binop",
    I64UnaryOp = "i64_unop",
    CmpI64 = "cmp_i64",
    NotTValue = "not_tvalue",
    FieldLoad = "table_field_load",
    FieldStore = "table_field_store",
    ArrayLoad = "table_array_load",
    ArrayStore = "table_array_store",
    BarrierCheck = "barrier_check",
    Call = "call_boundary",
    KnownCall = "call_boundary_known",
    TailCall = "tailcall_boundary",
    Return0 = "return0",
    Return1 = "return1",
    Boundary = "boundary",
    Jump = "jump",
    Branch = "branch",
}

M.CODEGEN_OP = CODEGEN_OP

local PURE_OP = {
    FrameLoad = true, LoadConst = true, ConstI64 = true, ConstF64 = true, ConstNil = true,
    ConstBool = true, Move = true, UnboxI64 = true, ToF64 = true, PowF64 = true, BoxI64 = true, NotTValue = true,
    AddI64 = true, SubI64 = true, MulI64 = true, DivI64ToF64 = true, I64BinOp = true, I64UnaryOp = true, CmpI64 = true,
    FieldLoad = true, ArrayLoad = true,
}

M.PURE_OP = PURE_OP

local HARD_BARRIER = {
    Call = true, KnownCall = true, TailCall = true, Boundary = true, Jump = true,
}
M.HARD_BARRIER = HARD_BARRIER

local Graph = {}
Graph.__index = Graph
M.Graph = Graph

function M.new(facts, config)
    return Graph.new(facts, config)
end

function Graph.new(facts, config)
    local factset = facts
    if getmetatable(facts) ~= Facts.FactSet then factset = Facts.new(facts or {}) end
    local g = setmetatable({
        values = {},
        nodes = {},
        memories = {},
        factset = factset,
        config = config or {},
        next_node = 1,
        next_value = 1,
        next_memory = 1,
        current = nil,
        rejected = false,
        rejections = {},
        stats = { guards = 0, removed = 0, barriers = 0, values = 0, memory_tokens = 0 },
    }, Graph)
    g.mem = {
        frame = g:new_memory("frame", "entry"),
        table = g:new_memory("table", "entry"),
        gc = g:new_memory("gc", "entry"),
        call = g:new_memory("call", "entry"),
    }
    return g
end

function Graph:new_memory(domain, reason)
    local id = "m" .. tostring(self.next_memory)
    self.next_memory = self.next_memory + 1
    local m = { id = id, domain = domain or "unknown", reason = reason or "" }
    self.memories[id] = m
    self.stats.memory_tokens = self.stats.memory_tokens + 1
    return id
end

function Graph:new_value(ty, name, facts, residency)
    ty = ty or "Unknown"
    if not VALUE_TYPES[ty] then ty = "Unknown" end
    local id = name or ("v" .. tostring(self.next_value))
    if not name then self.next_value = self.next_value + 1 end
    local v = { id = id, ty = ty, facts = copy_array(facts), residency = residency }
    self.values[id] = v
    self.stats.values = self.stats.values + 1
    return id
end

function Graph:value(v) return self.values[v] end

function Graph:add(op, t)
    t = t or {}
    local effect = t.effect or "none"
    if effect == "return" then effect = "return_" end
    if not EFFECTS[effect] then effect = "none" end
    local n = {
        id = self.next_node,
        op = op,
        codegen_op = t.codegen_op or CODEGEN_OP[op] or op,
        inputs = copy_array(t.inputs),
        outputs = copy_array(t.outputs),
        args = t.args or {},
        effect = effect,
        mem_in = t.mem_in or {},
        mem_out = t.mem_out or {},
        guard = t.guard,
        exit = t.exit,
        deps = copy_array(t.deps),
        source = t.source,
        removed = false,
        remove_reason = nil,
    }
    self.next_node = self.next_node + 1
    self.nodes[#self.nodes + 1] = n
    if effect == "guard" then self.stats.guards = self.stats.guards + 1 end
    if HARD_BARRIER[op] then self.stats.barriers = self.stats.barriers + 1 end
    return n
end

function Graph:exit_projection(reason, pc, live_slots, virtual_values)
    return {
        reason = reason or "guard_exit",
        pc = pc or 0,
        live_slots = copy_array(live_slots or { "cur" }),
        virtual_values = copy_array(virtual_values or {}),
        ok = true,
    }
end

function Graph:reject(opcode, pc, reason, event)
    self.rejected = true
    self.rejections[#self.rejections + 1] = {
        opcode = opcode,
        pc = pc or 0,
        reason = reason or "unsupported",
        event = event,
    }
    return true
end

function Graph:boundary(opcode, pc, event, reason)
    return self:add("Boundary", {
        source = pc,
        effect = "branch",
        args = { opcode = opcode, reason = reason or "vm_boundary", event = event },
        exit = self:exit_projection("boundary:" .. tostring(opcode), pc),
    })
end

function Graph:guard(subject, fact, op, pc)
    local guard_op = op
    if not guard_op then
        if fact.predicate == "is_i64" then guard_op = "GuardTypeI64"
        elseif fact.predicate == "is_number" or fact.predicate == "is_f64" then guard_op = "GuardNumber"
        elseif fact.predicate == "is_table" then guard_op = "GuardTable"
        elseif fact.predicate == "shape_known" or fact.predicate == "shape_eq" then guard_op = "GuardShape"
        elseif fact.predicate == "metatable_absent" then guard_op = "GuardMetatableAbsent"
        elseif fact.predicate == "known_call_target" or fact.predicate == "target_eq" then guard_op = "GuardCallTarget"
        elseif fact.predicate == "array_hit" then guard_op = "GuardArrayHit"
        elseif fact.predicate == "bounds_ok" then guard_op = "GuardBounds"
        else guard_op = "Guard" end
    end
    return self:add(guard_op, {
        inputs = { subject },
        source = pc,
        effect = "guard",
        guard = { fact = fact, key = Facts.guard_key(fact) },
        exit = self:exit_projection("guard:" .. tostring(fact.predicate), pc),
        deps = copy_array(fact.deps),
    })
end

function Graph:frame_load(slot, ty, pc)
    local out = self:new_value(ty or "TValue")
    self:add("FrameLoad", {
        outputs = { out }, source = pc, effect = "frame_read",
        mem_in = { frame = self.mem.frame }, args = { slot = slot or "cur" },
    })
    self.current = out
    return out
end

function Graph:frame_store(slot, value, pc)
    local newm = self:new_memory("frame", "store:" .. tostring(slot or "cur"))
    self:add("FrameStore", {
        inputs = { value }, source = pc, effect = "frame_write",
        mem_in = { frame = self.mem.frame }, mem_out = { frame = newm }, args = { slot = slot or "cur" },
    })
    self.mem.frame = newm
    self.current = value
end

function Graph:slot_value(slot)
    if self.current then return self.current end
    return self:frame_load(slot or "cur", "TValue")
end

function Graph:store_current(value, slot, pc)
    self:frame_store(slot or "cur", value, pc)
end

end

    self:add("BarrierCheck", {
        inputs = { owner, val }, source = pc, effect = "gc_barrier",
        mem_in = { gc = self.mem.gc }, mem_out = { gc = newm },
    })
    self.mem.gc = newm
end

function Graph:call(kind, fn, pc)
    local op = kind == "known" and "KnownCall" or "Call"
    local out = self:new_value("TValue")
    local new_call = self:new_memory("call", "call")
    self:add(op, {
        inputs = { fn }, outputs = { out }, source = pc, effect = "call",
        mem_in = { call = self.mem.call, frame = self.mem.frame, table = self.mem.table, gc = self.mem.gc },
        mem_out = { call = new_call },
        exit = self:exit_projection("call_exit", pc),
    })
    self.mem.call = new_call
    self.current = out
    return out
end

function Graph:return1(v, pc)
    self:add("Return1", { inputs = { v }, source = pc, effect = "return", exit = self:exit_projection("return", pc) })
end

function Graph:return0(pc)
    self:add("Return0", { source = pc, effect = "return", exit = self:exit_projection("return", pc) })
end

function Graph:jump(pc, ev)
    self:add("Jump", { source = pc, effect = "branch", args = { sj = ev and (ev.sj or ev.sbx or ev.sBx) }, exit = self:exit_projection("jump", pc) })
end

function Graph:active_nodes()
    local out = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(self.nodes) do if not n.removed then out[#out + 1] = n end end
    return out
end

function Graph:active_codegen_ops()
    local out = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(self.nodes) do if not n.removed and n.codegen_op then out[#out + 1] = n.codegen_op end end
    return out
end

function Graph:validate()
    local errors = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(self.nodes) do
        if not n.removed then
            if n.effect == "guard" and not n.exit then errors[#errors + 1] = "guard without exit at node " .. n.id end
            if n.op == "Boundary" and not n.exit then errors[#errors + 1] = "Boundary without exit at node " .. n.id end
            if n.effect == "call" and not n.exit then errors[#errors + 1] = "call without exit at node " .. n.id end
            for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do if not self.values[v] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown input " .. tostring(v) .. " at node " .. n.id end end
            for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do if not self.values[v] then errors[#errors + 1] = "unknown output " .. tostring(v) .. " at node " .. n.id end end
        end
    end
    return #errors == 0, errors
end

function Graph:canonical_lines()
    local lines = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(self.nodes) do
        if not n.removed then
            local args = {}
            for _, k in ipairs(sorted_keys(n.args or {})) do args[#args + 1] = tostring(k) .. "=" .. tostring(n.args[k]) end
            local guard = n.guard and n.guard.key or ""
            lines[#lines + 1] = table.concat({ n.op, table.concat(n.inputs or {}, ","), table.concat(n.outputs or {}, ","), n.effect, guard, table.concat(args, ",") }, ";")
        end
    end
    return lines
end

return M

-- ssa_lift.lua -- fact-consuming semantic lowering for PUC Lua opcode windows.
--
-- SSA here is the specialization boundary: opcode semantics + facts become a
-- simple canonical core graph. Missing facts or unsupported VM semantics become
-- structured rejection records; explicit VM control-transfer opcodes become
-- exact boundary handoffs.

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local IR = require("src.ssa_ir")
local Coverage = require("src.opcode_coverage")

local M = {}

local function factset_of(facts)
    if getmetatable(facts) == Facts.FactSet then return facts end
    return Facts.new(facts or {})
end

local function op_name(x) return type(x) == "table" and tostring(x.op or x.name or x[1] or "?") or tostring(x) end
local function slot_name(n) return n ~= nil and ("R" .. tostring(n)) or "cur" end
local function slot_subject(n) return Facts.slot(slot_name(n)) end
local function k_subject(k) return Facts.value("K" .. tostring(k or 0)) end
local function mkfact(kind, subject, predicate, value) return Facts.fact(kind, subject, predicate, value, "assumed") end

local function implies(g, predicate, subject, value)
    local ok, f = g.factset:implies(predicate, subject, value)
    if ok then return ok, f end
    if predicate == "is_i64" then return g.factset:implies("i64", subject, value) end
    if predicate == "is_table" then return g.factset:implies("table", subject, value) end
    return false, nil
end

local function has_global(g, predicate)
    return implies(g, predicate, Facts.global_subject(), true) or implies(g, predicate, Facts.global_subject())
end

local function has_slot(g, slot, predicate, roles)
    local ss = slot_subject(slot)
    local ok = implies(g, predicate, ss, true) or implies(g, predicate, ss)
    if ok then return true, ss end
    for _, role in ipairs(roles or {}) do
        local vs = Facts.value(role)
        ok = implies(g, predicate, vs, true) or implies(g, predicate, vs)
        if ok then return true, vs end
    end
    return false, ss
end

local function has_k(g, k, predicate)
    local ks = k_subject(k)
    local ok = implies(g, predicate, ks, true) or implies(g, predicate, ks)
    if ok then return true, ks end
    ok = implies(g, predicate, Facts.value("key"), true) or implies(g, predicate, Facts.value("key"))
    if ok then return true, Facts.value("key") end
    return false, ks
end

local function guard(g, value, f, op, pc) return g:guard(value, f, op, pc) end

local function guard_nonzero_i64(g, value, subject, pc)
    return guard(g, value, mkfact("value", subject or Facts.value("rhs"), "nonzero_i64", true), "GuardNonZeroI64", pc)
end

local function reject(g, opcode, pc, ev, reason)
    return g:reject(opcode, pc, reason or "unsupported_opcode_or_fact", ev)
end

local function boundary(g, opcode, pc, ev, reason)
    g:boundary(opcode, pc, ev, reason)
    return true
end

local function load_slot(g, slot, pc)
    return g:frame_load(slot_name(slot), "TValue", pc)
end

local function i64_from_slot(g, slot, role, pc)
    local ok, subj = has_slot(g, slot, "is_i64", { role or "value", "last" })
    if not ok then return nil end
    local tv = load_slot(g, slot, pc)
    guard(g, tv, mkfact("type", subj, "is_i64"), "GuardTypeI64", pc)
    return g:unbox_i64(tv, pc)
end

local function number_from_slot(g, slot, role, pc)
    local ok, subj = has_slot(g, slot, "is_number", { role or "value", "last" })
    if not ok then ok, subj = has_slot(g, slot, "is_i64", { role or "value", "last" }) end
    if not ok then ok, subj = has_slot(g, slot, "is_f64", { role or "value", "last" }) end
    if not ok then return nil end
    local tv = load_slot(g, slot, pc)
    guard(g, tv, mkfact("type", subj, "is_number"), "GuardNumber", pc)
    return g:to_f64(tv, pc)
end

local function number_from_const(g, k, pc)
    local ok, subj = has_k(g, k, "const_i64")
    if not ok then ok, subj = has_k(g, k, "const_f64") end
    if not ok then return nil end
    local tv = g:load_const("K" .. tostring(k or 0), pc)
    guard(g, tv, mkfact("type", subj, "is_number"), "GuardNumber", pc)
    return g:to_f64(tv, pc)
end

local function table_guards_for_value(g, tv, subj, pc, require_shape, require_meta, array_mode)
    guard(g, tv, mkfact("type", subj, "is_table"), "GuardTable", pc)
    if require_shape then
        local s_ok, s_subj = implies(g, "shape_known", subj, true) or implies(g, "shape_known", subj)
        if not s_ok then return nil end
        guard(g, tv, mkfact("shape", s_subj or subj, "shape_known", true), "GuardShape", pc)
    end
    if require_meta then
        local m_ok, m_subj = implies(g, "metatable_absent", subj, true) or implies(g, "metatable_absent", subj)
        if not m_ok then return nil end
        guard(g, tv, mkfact("metatable", m_subj or subj, "metatable_absent", true), "GuardMetatableAbsent", pc)
    end
    if array_mode then
        local a_ok, a_subj = implies(g, "array_hit", subj, true) or implies(g, "array_hit", subj)
        if not a_ok then return nil end
        guard(g, tv, mkfact("array", a_subj or subj, "array_hit", true), "GuardArrayHit", pc)
    end
    return tv
end

local function table_from_upvalue(g, index, role, pc, require_shape, require_meta, array_mode)
    local subj = Facts.value(role or ("upvalue" .. tostring(index or 0)))
    local ok = implies(g, "is_table", subj, true) or implies(g, "is_table", subj)
    if not ok then ok = implies(g, "is_table", Facts.value("table"), true) or implies(g, "is_table", Facts.value("table")) end
    if not ok then return nil end
    local tv = g:upvalue_load(index or 0, pc)
    return table_guards_for_value(g, tv, subj, pc, require_shape, require_meta, array_mode)
end

local function table_from_slot(g, slot, role, pc, require_shape, require_meta, array_mode)
    local ok, subj = has_slot(g, slot, "is_table", { role or "table", "table" })
    if not ok then return nil end
    local tv = load_slot(g, slot, pc)
    guard(g, tv, mkfact("type", subj, "is_table"), "GuardTable", pc)
    if require_shape then
        local s_ok, s_subj = has_slot(g, slot, "shape_known", { role or "table", "table" })
        if not s_ok then return nil end
        guard(g, tv, mkfact("shape", s_subj, "shape_known", true), "GuardShape", pc)
    end
    if require_meta then
        local m_ok, m_subj = has_slot(g, slot, "metatable_absent", { role or "table", "table" })
        if not m_ok then return nil end
        guard(g, tv, mkfact("metatable", m_subj, "metatable_absent", true), "GuardMetatableAbsent", pc)
    end
    if array_mode then
        local a_ok, a_subj = has_slot(g, slot, "array_hit", { role or "table", "table" })
        if not a_ok then return nil end
        guard(g, tv, mkfact("array", a_subj, "array_hit", true), "GuardArrayHit", pc)
    end
    return tv
end

local function box_store_i64(g, v, dst, pc)
    local boxed = g:box_i64(v, pc)
    g:store_current(boxed, slot_name(dst), pc)
end

local function store_tvalue(g, v, dst, pc)
    g:store_current(v, slot_name(dst), pc)
end

local BIN_RR = { ADD=true, SUB=true, MUL=true, DIV=true, MOD=true, IDIV=true, BAND=true, BOR=true, BXOR=true, SHL=true, SHR=true, POW=true }
local BIN_RI = { ADDI=true, SHLI=true, SHRI=true }
local BIN_K = { ADDK=true, SUBK=true, MULK=true, MODK=true, POWK=true, DIVK=true, IDIVK=true, BANDK=true, BORK=true, BXORK=true }
local CMP_RR = { EQ=true, LT=true, LE=true }
local CMP_RI = { EQI=true, LTI=true, LEI=true, GTI=true, GEI=true }
local CMP_K = { EQK=true }

local K_TO_BIN = { ADDK="ADD", SUBK="SUB", MULK="MUL", MODK="MOD", DIVK="DIV", IDIVK="IDIV", BANDK="BAND", BORK="BOR", BXORK="BXOR", POWK="POW" }

local NUMERIC_FOR = { FORPREP = true, FORLOOP = true }
local GENERIC_FOR = { TFORPREP = true, TFORCALL = true, TFORLOOP = true }

local BOUNDARY_REASON = {
    LOADKX = "extraarg_boundary",
    LFALSESKIP = "branch_skip_boundary",
    EQ = "comparison_boundary", LT = "comparison_boundary", LE = "comparison_boundary", EQK = "comparison_boundary",
    EQI = "comparison_boundary", LTI = "comparison_boundary", LEI = "comparison_boundary", GTI = "comparison_boundary", GEI = "comparison_boundary",
    TEST = "test_branch_boundary", TESTSET = "test_branch_boundary",
    MMBIN = "metamethod_boundary", MMBINI = "metamethod_boundary", MMBINK = "metamethod_boundary",
    LEN = "length_boundary", CONCAT = "concat_boundary",
    CLOSE = "close_boundary", TBC = "tbc_boundary",
    NEWTABLE = "allocation_boundary", SETLIST = "bulk_table_boundary", CLOSURE = "closure_boundary",
    VARARG = "vararg_boundary", GETVARG = "vararg_boundary", VARARGPREP = "vararg_boundary",
    ERRNNIL = "error_boundary", EXTRAARG = "extraarg_boundary",
}

local function lower_loop_region_boundary(g, op, pc, ev)
    local reason = NUMERIC_FOR[op] and "numeric_for_region_boundary" or "generic_for_region_boundary"
    return boundary(g, op, pc, ev, reason)
end

local function const_k_i64(g, k, pc)
    local ok = has_k(g, k, "const_i64")
    if not ok then return nil end
    return g:const_i64(0, pc) -- runtime value is patched by HOLE in C emitter when sourced from K op
end

local function lower_pow(g, op, pc, ev)
    local lhs = number_from_slot(g, ev.b, "lhs", pc)
    if not lhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_lhs_number_fact") end
    local rhs
    if op == "POWK" then
        rhs = number_from_const(g, ev.c or ev.bx or 0, pc)
        if not rhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_const_number_fact") end
    else
        rhs = number_from_slot(g, ev.c, "rhs", pc)
        if not rhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_rhs_number_fact") end
    end
    local out = g:pow_f64(lhs, rhs, pc)
    store_tvalue(g, out, ev.a, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_i64_bin(g, op, pc, ev)
    if op == "POW" or op == "POWK" then return lower_pow(g, op, pc, ev) end
    local lhs, rhs, real_op = nil, nil, op
    if op == "SHLI" then
        rhs = i64_from_slot(g, ev.b, "rhs", pc)
        if not rhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_rhs_i64_fact") end
        lhs = g:const_i64(ev.sc or ev.sC or ev.sb or ev.sB or ev.c or 0, pc)
        real_op = "SHL"
    else
        lhs = i64_from_slot(g, ev.b, "lhs", pc)
        if not lhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_lhs_i64_fact") end
        if op == "SHRI" then
            rhs = g:const_i64(ev.sc or ev.sC or ev.sb or ev.sB or ev.c or 0, pc)
            real_op = "SHR"
        elseif BIN_RI[op] then
            rhs = g:const_i64(ev.sc or ev.sC or ev.sb or ev.sB or ev.c or 0, pc)
            real_op = (op == "ADDI") and "ADD" or op
        elseif BIN_K[op] then
            rhs = const_k_i64(g, ev.c or ev.bx or 0, pc)
            if not rhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_const_i64_fact") end
            real_op = K_TO_BIN[op] or op
        else
            rhs = i64_from_slot(g, ev.c, "rhs", pc)
            if not rhs then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_rhs_i64_fact") end
        end
    end
    local supported_i64_bin = { ADD = true, SUB = true, MUL = true, MOD = true, DIV = true, IDIV = true, BAND = true, BOR = true, BXOR = true, SHL = true, SHR = true }
    if not supported_i64_bin[real_op] then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "i64_op_not_semantically_lowered") end
    local native
    if real_op == "DIV" then
        native = g:i64_div_to_f64(lhs, rhs, pc)
        store_tvalue(g, native, ev.a, pc)
        return false
    end
    if real_op == "MOD" or real_op == "IDIV" then
        guard_nonzero_i64(g, rhs, Facts.value("rhs"), pc)
        native = g:i64_binop(real_op, lhs, rhs, pc)
        box_store_i64(g, native, ev.a, pc)
        return false
    end
    if real_op == "ADD" then native = g:i64_arith("ADD", lhs, rhs, pc)
    elseif real_op == "SUB" then native = g:i64_arith("SUB", lhs, rhs, pc)
    elseif real_op == "MUL" then native = g:i64_arith("MUL", lhs, rhs, pc)
    else native = g:i64_binop(real_op, lhs, rhs, pc) end
    box_store_i64(g, native, ev.a, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_i64_cmp(g, op, pc, ev)
    return boundary(g, op, pc, ev, "comparison_boundary")
end

local function lower_i64_unary(g, op, pc, ev)
    if op == "NOT" then
        local v = load_slot(g, ev.b, pc)
        local out = g:not_tvalue(v, pc)
        store_tvalue(g, out, ev.a, pc)
        return false
    end
    if op == "LEN" then return boundary(g, op, pc, ev, BOUNDARY_REASON[op]) end
    if op ~= "UNM" and op ~= "BNOT" then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "generic_unary") end
    local x = i64_from_slot(g, ev.b, "arg", pc)
    if not x then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_arg_i64_fact") end

[187 more lines in file. Use offset=281 to continue.]

local out = g:i64_unop(op, x, pc)
    box_store_i64(g, out, ev.a, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_load(g, op, pc, ev)
    if op == "LOADI" then
        box_store_i64(g, g:const_i64(ev.sbx or ev.sBx or ev.bx or 0, pc), ev.a, pc)
    elseif op == "LOADF" then
        store_tvalue(g, g:const_f64(ev.sbx or ev.sBx or ev.bx or 0, pc), ev.a, pc)
    elseif op == "LOADTRUE" or op == "LOADFALSE" then
        store_tvalue(g, g:const_bool(op == "LOADTRUE", pc), ev.a, pc)
    elseif op == "LFALSESKIP" then
        return boundary(g, op, pc, ev, "branch_skip_boundary")
    elseif op == "LOADNIL" then
        store_tvalue(g, g:const_nil(pc), ev.a, pc)
    elseif op == "LOADK" then
        store_tvalue(g, g:load_const("K" .. tostring(ev.bx or ev.ax or ev.b or 0), pc), ev.a, pc)
    elseif op == "LOADKX" then
        return boundary(g, op, pc, ev, "extraarg_boundary")
    else
        return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "load_form_not_lowered")
    end
    return false
end

local function lower_move(g, pc, ev)
    local v = load_slot(g, ev.b, pc)
    local out = g:new_value("TValue")
    g:add("Move", { inputs = { v }, outputs = { out }, source = pc })
    store_tvalue(g, out, ev.a, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_field_get(g, op, pc, ev)
    local tab, shape_payload
    if op == "GETTABUP" then
        shape_payload = has_k(g, ev.c or 0, "field_offset")
        if not shape_payload then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_shape_payload_fact") end
        tab = table_from_upvalue(g, ev.b or 0, "table", pc, true, true, false)
    else
        shape_payload = has_slot(g, ev.b, "shape_eq", { "shape", "table" })
        if not shape_payload then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_shape_payload_fact") end
        tab = table_from_slot(g, ev.b, "table", pc, true, true, false)
    end
    if not tab then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_table_shape_facts") end
    local key_ok = has_k(g, ev.c or 0, "key_const")
    if not key_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_key_const_fact") end
    local off_ok = has_k(g, ev.c or 0, "field_offset")
    if not off_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_field_offset_fact") end
    local v = g:field_load(tab, "K" .. tostring(ev.c or 0), pc)
    store_tvalue(g, v, ev.a, pc)
    if op == "SELF" then
        local recv = load_slot(g, ev.b, pc)
        store_tvalue(g, recv, (ev.a or 0) + 1, pc)
    end
    return false
end

local function maybe_barrier(g, tab, val, pc)
    if not has_global(g, "barrier_clean") then g:barrier(tab, val, pc) end
end

local function rk_value(g, idx, pc, ev)
    if ev and (ev.k or ev.c_is_k or ev.isk or ev.rk == "K") then return g:load_const("K" .. tostring(idx or 0), pc) end
    return load_slot(g, idx, pc)
end

local function lower_upvalue(g, op, pc, ev)
    if op == "GETUPVAL" then
        local v = g:upvalue_load(ev.b or 0, pc)
        store_tvalue(g, v, ev.a, pc)
    else
        local owner = g:upvalue_load(ev.b or 0, pc)
        local val = load_slot(g, ev.a, pc)
        g:upvalue_store(ev.b or 0, val, pc)
        maybe_barrier(g, owner, val, pc)
    end
    return false
end

local function lower_field_set(g, op, pc, ev)
    local tab, shape_payload, key_index
    if op == "SETTABUP" then
        key_index = ev.b or 0
        shape_payload = has_k(g, key_index, "field_offset")
        if not shape_payload then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_shape_payload_fact") end
        tab = table_from_upvalue(g, ev.a or 0, "table", pc, true, true, false)
    else
        key_index = ev.b or 0
        shape_payload = has_slot(g, ev.a, "shape_eq", { "shape", "table" })
        if not shape_payload then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_shape_payload_fact") end
        tab = table_from_slot(g, ev.a, "table", pc, true, true, false)
    end
    if not tab then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_table_shape_facts") end
    local key_ok = has_k(g, key_index, "key_const")
    if not key_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_key_const_fact") end
    local off_ok = has_k(g, key_index, "field_offset")
    if not off_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_field_offset_fact") end
    local val = rk_value(g, ev.c, pc, ev)
    g:field_store(tab, "K" .. tostring(key_index), val, pc)
    maybe_barrier(g, tab, val, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_array_get(g, op, pc, ev)
    local tab = table_from_slot(g, ev.b, "table", pc, false, true, true)
    if not tab then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_array_facts") end
    local base_ok = has_slot(g, ev.b, "array_base_offset", { "table" })
    if not base_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_array_base_fact") end
    local len_ok = has_slot(g, ev.b, "array_len_offset", { "table" })
    if not len_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_array_len_fact") end
    local idx
    if op == "GETI" then
        idx = g:const_i64(ev.c or 0, pc)
    else
        idx = i64_from_slot(g, ev.c, "key", pc)
        if not idx then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_key_i64_fact") end
    end
    guard(g, tab, mkfact("array", slot_subject(ev.b), "bounds_ok", true), "GuardBounds", pc).inputs = { tab, idx }
    local v = g:array_load(tab, idx, pc)
    store_tvalue(g, v, ev.a, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_array_set(g, op, pc, ev)
    local tab = table_from_slot(g, ev.a, "table", pc, false, true, true)
    if not tab then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_array_facts") end
    local base_ok = has_slot(g, ev.a, "array_base_offset", { "table" })
    if not base_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_array_base_fact") end
    local len_ok = has_slot(g, ev.a, "array_len_offset", { "table" })
    if not len_ok then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_array_len_fact") end
    local idx
    if op == "SETI" then
        idx = g:const_i64(ev.b or 0, pc)
    else
        idx = i64_from_slot(g, ev.b, "key", pc)
        if not idx then return reject(g, op, pc, ev, "missing_key_i64_fact") end
    end
    guard(g, tab, mkfact("array", slot_subject(ev.a), "bounds_ok", true), "GuardBounds", pc).inputs = { tab, idx }
    local val = rk_value(g, ev.c, pc, ev)
    g:array_store(tab, idx, val, pc)
    maybe_barrier(g, tab, val, pc)
    return false
end

local function lower_call(g, op, pc, ev)
    return boundary(g, op, pc, ev, "call_boundary")
end

function M.lift(ops, facts, config)
    local fs = factset_of(facts)
    local g = IR.new(fs, config)
    if not fs:ok() then
        g.invalid = true
        g.invalid_reasons = fs:contradictions()
        return g
    end

    for pc, ev in ipairs(ops or {}) do
        local op = op_name(ev)
        local cov = Coverage.classify(op)
        local terminal = false
        if op == "MOVE" then terminal = lower_move(g, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "LOADI" or op == "LOADF" or op == "LOADTRUE" or op == "LOADFALSE" or op == "LFALSESKIP" or op == "LOADNIL" or op == "LOADK" or op == "LOADKX" then terminal = lower_load(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif BIN_RR[op] or BIN_RI[op] or BIN_K[op] then terminal = lower_i64_bin(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif CMP_RR[op] or CMP_RI[op] or CMP_K[op] then terminal = lower_i64_cmp(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "UNM" or op == "BNOT" or op == "NOT" or op == "LEN" then terminal = lower_i64_unary(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "GETUPVAL" or op == "SETUPVAL" then terminal = lower_upvalue(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "GETFIELD" or op == "GETTABUP" or op == "SELF" then terminal = lower_field_get(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "SETFIELD" or op == "SETTABUP" then terminal = lower_field_set(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "GETTABLE" or op == "GETI" then terminal = lower_array_get(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "SETTABLE" or op == "SETI" then terminal = lower_array_set(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "CALL" or op == "TAILCALL" then terminal = lower_call(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif op == "RETURN0" then g:return0(pc); terminal = true
        elseif op == "RETURN" or op == "RETURN1" then g:return1(load_slot(g, ev.a, pc), pc); terminal = true
        elseif op == "JMP" then g:jump(pc, ev); terminal = true
        elseif NUMERIC_FOR[op] or GENERIC_FOR[op] then terminal = lower_loop_region_boundary(g, op, pc, ev)
        elseif cov.mode == Coverage.MODE.boundary then terminal = boundary(g, op, pc, ev, BOUNDARY_REASON[op] or "vm_boundary")
        else terminal = reject(g, op, pc, ev, "opcode_not_supported") end
        if terminal or g.rejected then break end
    end
    return g
end

return M

-- facts.lua -- typed fact lattice for the SponJIT foundry.
--
-- Strings are accepted only as boundary shorthand for corpus/enumerator input;
-- inside the optimizer facts are records with subject/predicate/value/source/deps.

local Util = require("src.util")

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
    local out = {}
    for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
    return out
end

local function sorted_keys(t)
    local out = {}
    for k in pairs(t or {}) do out[#out + 1] = k end
    table.sort(out)
    return out
end

local function value_key(v)
    if v == nil then return "" end
    if type(v) ~= "table" then return tostring(v) end
    local parts = {}
    for _, k in ipairs(sorted_keys(v)) do parts[#parts + 1] = tostring(k) .. "=" .. value_key(v[k]) end
    return "{" .. table.concat(parts, ",") .. "}"
end

function M.subject(kind, id, attrs)
    return { kind = kind, id = tostring(id or "?"), attrs = attrs or {} }
end

function M.value(id) return M.subject("value", id) end
function M.slot(id) return M.subject("slot", id) end
function M.table_ref(id) return M.subject("table", id) end
function M.callsite(id) return M.subject("callsite", id) end
function M.pc(id) return M.subject("pc", id) end
function M.memory(id) return M.subject("memory", id) end
function M.global_subject() return M.subject("global", "*") end

function M.subject_key(s)
    if type(s) == "string" then return s end
    if type(s) ~= "table" then return tostring(s) end
    return tostring(s.kind or "?") .. ":" .. tostring(s.id or "?")
end

local DEP_BY_PREDICATE = {
    shape_eq = { "shape_epoch" },
    shape_known = { "shape_epoch" },
    field_offset = { "shape_epoch" },
    metatable_absent = { "metatable_epoch" },
    no_metamethod = { "metatable_epoch" },
    target_eq = { "call_target_epoch" },
    known_call_target = { "call_target_epoch" },
    barrier_clean = { "gc_barrier_protocol" },
    array_hit = { "array_epoch" },
    bounds_ok = { "array_epoch" },
}

local TYPE_PREDICATES = {
    is_nil = true, is_bool = true, is_i64 = true, is_f64 = true,
    is_number = true, is_table = true, is_closure = true, is_string = true,
}

local IMPLIED_BY_PREDICATE = {
    shape_known = { { predicate = "is_table" } },
    shape_eq = { { predicate = "is_table" }, { predicate = "shape_known" } },
    -- field_offset is a key/shape payload lease. Its subject is usually K<n>,
    -- so it must not imply is_table (that belongs on the table slot).
    field_offset = { { predicate = "key_const" } },
    metatable_absent = { { predicate = "no_metamethod", value = "__index" }, { predicate = "no_metamethod", value = "__newindex" } },
    array_hit = { { predicate = "is_table" } },
    bounds_ok = { { predicate = "array_hit" } },
    array_base_offset = { { predicate = "array_hit" }, { predicate = "bounds_ok" } },
    array_len_offset = { { predicate = "array_hit" }, { predicate = "bounds_ok" } },
    target_eq = { { predicate = "known_call_target" }, { predicate = "is_closure" } },
}

local SHORTHAND = {
    lhs_i64 = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("lhs"), "is_i64", nil, "observed") end,
    rhs_i64 = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("rhs"), "is_i64", nil, "observed") end,
    lhs_number = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("lhs"), "is_number", nil, "observed") end,
    rhs_number = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("rhs"), "is_number", nil, "observed") end,
    last_i64 = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("last"), "is_i64", nil, "observed") end,
    key_i64 = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("key"), "key_i64", nil, "observed") end,
    table = function() return M.fact("type", M.value("table"), "is_table", nil, "observed") end,
    shape_known = function() return M.fact("shape", M.value("table"), "shape_known", true, "observed") end,
    metatable_absent = function() return M.fact("metatable", M.value("table"), "metatable_absent", true, "observed") end,
    key_const = function() return M.fact("constant", M.value("key"), "key_const", true, "observed") end,
    array_hit = function() return M.fact("array", M.value("table"), "array_hit", true, "observed") end,
    bounds = function() return M.fact("array", M.value("table"), "bounds_ok", true, "observed") end,
    array_bounds_known = function() return M.fact("array", M.value("table"), "bounds_ok", true, "observed") end,
    known_call_target = function() return M.fact("call", M.value("callee"), "known_call_target", true, "observed") end,
    result_known_call = function() return M.fact("call", M.value("last"), "known_call_target", true, "observed") end,
    callee_from_prev = function() return M.fact("call", M.value("callee"), "from_prev", true, "observed") end,
    barrier_clean = function() return M.fact("gc", M.global_subject(), "barrier_clean", true, "observed") end,
    returns_prev = function() return M.fact("liveness", M.value("last"), "returned", true, "observed") end,
    branch_consumes_prev = function() return M.fact("control", M.value("last"), "branch_consumed", true, "observed") end,
    loop_backedge = function() return M.fact("control", M.pc("backedge"), "loop_backedge", true, "observed") end,
    lhs_from_prev = function() return M.fact("flow", M.value("lhs"), "from_prev", true, "observed") end,
    result_dead = function() return M.fact("liveness", M.value("last"), "dead", true, "observed") end,
    slot_reuse = function() return M.fact("liveness", M.slot("cur"), "slot_reuse", true, "observed") end,
    value_in_rax = function() return M.fact("residency", M.value("last"), "in_reg", "rax", "observed") end,
    value_in_rcx = function() return M.fact("residency", M.value("last"), "in_reg", "rcx", "observed") end,
}

function M.fact(kind, subject, predicate, value, source, confidence, deps)
    local f = {
        kind = kind or "generic",
        subject = subject or M.global_subject(),
        predicate = predicate or "true",
        value = value,
        source = source or "assumed",
        confidence = confidence or "assumed",
        deps = copy_array(deps),
    }
    if #f.deps == 0 and DEP_BY_PREDICATE[f.predicate] then f.deps = copy_array(DEP_BY_PREDICATE[f.predicate]) end
    return f
end

function M.key(f)
    return table.concat({
        tostring(f.kind or "generic"),
        M.subject_key(f.subject),
        tostring(f.predicate or "true"),
        value_key(f.value),
    }, ":")
end

function M.guard_key(f)
    return M.subject_key(f.subject) .. ":" .. tostring(f.predicate) .. ":" .. value_key(f.value)
end

function M.parse(x)
    if type(x) == "table" and x.predicate then
        local f = {}
        for k, v in pairs(x) do f[k] = v end
        if f.predicate == "i64" then f.predicate = "is_i64" end
        if f.predicate == "table" then f.predicate = "is_table" end
        if type(f.subject) == "table" and not f.subject.kind and f.subject.id then
            local id = tostring(f.subject.id)
            if id:match("^R%d+$") then f.subject = M.slot(id) else f.subject = M.value(id) end
        elseif type(f.subject) == "string" then
            if f.subject:match("^R%d+$") then f.subject = M.slot(f.subject) else f.subject = M.value(f.subject) end
        end
        return M.fact(f.kind, f.subject, f.predicate, f.value, f.source, f.confidence, f.deps)
    end
    if type(x) == "string" and SHORTHAND[x] then return SHORTHAND[x]() end
    if type(x) == "string" then return M.fact("shorthand", M.global_subject(), x, true, "observed") end
    return M.fact("unknown", M.global_subject(), tostring(x), true, "observed")
end

local FactSet = {}
FactSet.__index = FactSet
M.FactSet = FactSet

function M.new(xs)
    local fs = setmetatable({ by_key = {}, order = {}, contradiction_list = {} }, FactSet)
    for _, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do fs:add(x) end
    fs:close()
    return fs
end

function FactSet:add(x)
    local f = M.parse(x)
    local k = M.key(f)
    if not self.by_key[k] then
        self.by_key[k] = f
        self.order[#self.order + 1] = k
    end
    return self.by_key[k]
end

function FactSet:each()
    local i = 0
    return function()
        i = i + 1
        local k = self.order[i]
        if k then return self.by_key[k] end
    end
end

function FactSet:has(predicate, subject, value)
    local sk = subject and M.subject_key(subject)
    for f in self:each() do
        if f.predicate == predicate and (not sk or M.subject_key(f.subject) == sk) and (value == nil or value_key(f.value) == value_key(value)) then
            return true, f
        end
    end
    return false
end

function FactSet:implies(x_or_predicate, subject, value)
    if type(x_or_predicate) == "table" and x_or_predicate.predicate then
        return self:has(x_or_predicate.predicate, x_or_predicate.subject, x_or_predicate.value)
    end
    return self:has(x_or_predicate, subject, value)
end

function FactSet:close()
    local changed = true
    while changed do
        changed = false
        local snapshot = {}
        for f in self:each() do snapshot[#snapshot + 1] = f end
        for _, f in ipairs(snapshot) do
            for _, imp in ipairs(IMPLIED_BY_PREDICATE[f.predicate] or {}) do
                local nf = M.fact(imp.kind or f.kind, f.subject, imp.predicate, imp.value, "implied", "proven", f.deps)
                local k = M.key(nf)
                if not self.by_key[k] then self:add(nf); changed = true end
            end
        end
    end
    self:check_contradictions()
    return self
end

function FactSet:check_contradictions()
    self.contradiction_list = {}
    local by_subject_type = {}
    local by_subject_shape = {}
    for f in self:each() do
        local sk = M.subject_key(f.subject)
        if TYPE_PREDICATES[f.predicate] then
            by_subject_type[sk] = by_subject_type[sk] or {}
            by_subject_type[sk][f.predicate] = true
        end
        if f.predicate == "shape_eq" then
            if by_subject_shape[sk] and value_key(by_subject_shape[sk]) ~= value_key(f.value) then
                self.contradiction_list[#self.contradiction_list + 1] = "conflicting shapes for " .. sk
            end
            by_subject_shape[sk] = f.value
        end
    end
    for sk, ts in pairs(by_subject_type) do
        local concrete = {}
        for t in pairs(ts) do if t ~= "is_number" then concrete[#concrete + 1] = t end end
        if #concrete > 1 then
            table.sort(concrete)
            self.contradiction_list[#self.contradiction_list + 1] = "conflicting types for " .. sk .. ": " .. table.concat(concrete, ",")
        end
    end
    return self.contradiction_list
end

function FactSet:contradictions() return copy_array(self.contradiction_list) end
function FactSet:ok() return #(self.contradiction_list or {}) == 0 end

function FactSet:guards_required()
    local out = {}
    for f in self:each() do
        if f.source ~= "static" and f.source ~= "implied" then out[#out + 1] = f end
    end
    table.sort(out, function(a, b) return M.key(a) < M.key(b) end)
    return out
end

function FactSet:deps()
    local seen, out = {}, {}
    for f in self:each() do
        for _, d in ipairs(f.deps or {}) do if not seen[d] then seen[d] = true; out[#out + 1] = d end end
    end
    table.sort(out)
    return out
end

function FactSet:list()
    local out = {}
    for f in self:each() do out[#out + 1] = f end
    table.sort(out, function(a, b) return M.key(a) < M.key(b) end)
    return out
end

function FactSet:canonical_key()
    local parts = {}
    for _, f in ipairs(self:list()) do parts[#parts + 1] = M.key(f) end
    return table.concat(parts, "|")
end

function FactSet:hash() return Util.stable_hash(self:canonical_key()) end

function FactSet:project(subjects)
    local want = {}
    for _, s in ipairs(subjects or {}) do want[M.subject_key(s)] = true end
    local out = M.new()
    for f in self:each() do if want[M.subject_key(f.subject)] then out:add(f) end end
    return out:close()
end

function FactSet:merge(other)
    local out = M.new(self:list())
    for f in (other or M.new()):each() do out:add(f) end
    return out:close()
end

function FactSet:shorthand_checked()
    local out = {}
    for _, f in ipairs(self:guards_required()) do out[#out + 1] = f.predicate end

    return out

-- ssa_fact_axes.lua -- curated fact-axis source for SponJIT foundry SSA.
--
-- This module is the contract between grammar enumeration, SSA lowering, bank
-- selection, and runtime patching. It deliberately does not enumerate a blind
-- powerset first. Instead it builds semantic bundles: facts that must travel
-- together for a lowering to be executable (type + guard lease + payload lease).
-- `subsets` then emits a bounded, ordered candidate ladder from those bundles.

local bit = require("bit")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local BIN_RR = { ADD=true, SUB=true, MUL=true, DIV=true, MOD=true, POW=true, IDIV=true,
  BAND=true, BOR=true, BXOR=true, SHL=true, SHR=true }
local BIN_RI = { ADDI=true, SHLI=true, SHRI=true }
local BIN_K = { ADDK=true, SUBK=true, MULK=true, MODK=true, POWK=true,
  DIVK=true, IDIVK=true, BANDK=true, BORK=true, BXORK=true }
local UNARY_I64 = { UNM=true, BNOT=true }
local CMP_RR = { EQ=true, LT=true, LE=true }
local CMP_RI = { EQI=true, LTI=true, LEI=true, GTI=true, GEI=true }
local CMP_K = { EQK=true }
local FIELD_GET = { GETFIELD=true, GETTABUP=true, SELF=true }
local FIELD_SET = { SETFIELD=true, SETTABUP=true }
local ARRAY_GET = { GETTABLE=true, GETI=true }
local ARRAY_SET = { SETTABLE=true, SETI=true }
local CALL = { CALL=true, TAILCALL=true }

local function opname(op) return type(op) == "table" and tostring(op.op) or tostring(op) end
local function reg(n) return n ~= nil and ("R" .. tostring(n)) or nil end
local function kval(k) return "K" .. tostring(k or 0) end
local function pc_id(pc) return "pc" .. tostring(pc or 0) end

local function axis_key(ax)
  local subj = ax.global and "*" or tostring(ax.slot or ax.subject or "?")
  local val = ax.value == nil and "" or tostring(ax.value)
  return subj .. ":" .. tostring(ax.predicate) .. ":" .. val
end

local function add_axis(out, seen, ax)
  if not ax or not ax.predicate then return nil end
  local k = axis_key(ax)
  if not seen[k] then
    seen[k] = ax
    out[#out + 1] = ax
  end
  return seen[k]
end

local function ax_slot(slot, predicate, attrs)
  attrs = attrs or {}
  attrs.slot = slot
  attrs.predicate = predicate
  return attrs
end
local function ax_k(k, predicate, attrs)
  attrs = attrs or {}
  attrs.slot = kval(k)
  attrs.predicate = predicate
  return attrs
end
local function ax_global(predicate, attrs)
  attrs = attrs or {}
  attrs.global = true
  attrs.predicate = predicate
  return attrs
end

local function make_bundle(bundles, name, axes, attrs)
  attrs = attrs or {}
  bundles[#bundles + 1] = {
    name = name,
    axes = axes,
    tier = attrs.tier or 1,
    pc = attrs.pc,
    op = attrs.op,
  }
end

local function bundle_numeric_rr(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local axes
  if n == "POW" then
    axes = { ax_slot(reg(op.b), "is_number", {role="lhs"}), ax_slot(reg(op.c), "is_number", {role="rhs"}) }
  else
    axes = { ax_slot(reg(op.b), "is_i64", {role="lhs"}), ax_slot(reg(op.c), "is_i64", {role="rhs"}) }
  end
  make_bundle(bundles, "num_rr:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, axes, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_numeric_ri(bundles, n, op, pc)
  make_bundle(bundles, "num_ri:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(reg(op.b), "is_i64", {role="lhs"}),
  }, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_numeric_k(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local k = op.c or op.bx or 0
  local pred = n == "POWK" and "is_number" or "is_i64"
  local axes = {
    ax_slot(reg(op.b), pred, {role="lhs"}),
    ax_k(k, "const_i64", {role="const", value="payload"}),
  }
  make_bundle(bundles, "num_k:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, axes, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_unary(bundles, n, op, pc)
  make_bundle(bundles, "unary:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(reg(op.b), "is_i64", {role="arg"}),
  }, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_cmp_rr(bundles, n, op, pc)
  make_bundle(bundles, "cmp_rr:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(reg(op.a), "is_i64", {role="lhs"}),
    ax_slot(reg(op.b), "is_i64", {role="rhs"}),
  }, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_cmp_ri(bundles, n, op, pc)
  make_bundle(bundles, "cmp_ri:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(reg(op.a), "is_i64", {role="lhs"}),
  }, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_cmp_k(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local k = op.bx or op.b or 0
  make_bundle(bundles, "cmp_k:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(reg(op.a), "is_i64", {role="lhs"}),
    ax_k(k, "const_i64", {role="const", value="payload"}),
  }, {tier=2, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_field_get(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local table_slot = (n == "GETTABUP") and "table" or reg(op.b)
  local k = op.c or 0
  -- Complete field lease: table identity, shape guard, metatable guard,
  -- key payload, field-offset payload. shape_eq/field_offset are payload facts;
  -- shape_known/key_const are cheap coarse facts used by selector/lifter.
  make_bundle(bundles, "field_get:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(table_slot, "is_table", {role="table"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "shape_known", {role="shape"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "shape_eq", {role="shape", value=pc_id(pc)}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "metatable_absent", {role="metatable"}),
    ax_k(k, "key_const", {role="key"}),
    ax_k(k, "field_offset", {role="field_offset", value=pc_id(pc)}),
  }, {tier=3, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_field_set(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local table_slot = (n == "SETTABUP") and "table" or reg(op.a)
  local k = op.b or 0
  local axes = {
    ax_slot(table_slot, "is_table", {role="table"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "shape_known", {role="shape"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "shape_eq", {role="shape", value=pc_id(pc)}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "metatable_absent", {role="metatable"}),
    ax_k(k, "key_const", {role="key"}),
    ax_k(k, "field_offset", {role="field_offset", value=pc_id(pc)}),
  }
  make_bundle(bundles, "field_set:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, axes, {tier=3, pc=pc, op=n})
  local clean = {}
  for i, ax in ipairs(axes) do clean[i] = ax end
  clean[#clean + 1] = ax_global("barrier_clean", {role="barrier"})
  make_bundle(bundles, "field_set_clean:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, clean, {tier=3, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_array_get(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local table_slot = reg(op.b)
  local axes = {
    ax_slot(table_slot, "is_table", {role="table"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "metatable_absent", {role="metatable"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "array_hit", {role="array"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "bounds_ok", {role="bounds"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "array_base_offset", {role="array_payload", value=pc_id(pc)}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "array_len_offset", {role="array_payload", value=pc_id(pc)}),
  }
  if n == "GETTABLE" then
    axes[#axes + 1] = ax_slot(reg(op.c), "is_i64", {role="key"})
    axes[#axes + 1] = ax_slot(reg(op.c), "key_i64", {role="key"})
  end
  make_bundle(bundles, "array_get:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, axes, {tier=3, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_array_set(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local table_slot = reg(op.a)
  local axes = {
    ax_slot(table_slot, "is_table", {role="table"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "metatable_absent", {role="metatable"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "array_hit", {role="array"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "bounds_ok", {role="bounds"}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "array_base_offset", {role="array_payload", value=pc_id(pc)}),
    ax_slot(table_slot, "array_len_offset", {role="array_payload", value=pc_id(pc)}),
  }
  if n == "SETTABLE" then
    axes[#axes + 1] = ax_slot(reg(op.b), "is_i64", {role="key"})
    axes[#axes + 1] = ax_slot(reg(op.b), "key_i64", {role="key"})
  end
  make_bundle(bundles, "array_set:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, axes, {tier=3, pc=pc, op=n})
  local clean = {}
  for i, ax in ipairs(axes) do clean[i] = ax end
  clean[#clean + 1] = ax_global("barrier_clean", {role="barrier"})
  make_bundle(bundles, "array_set_clean:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, clean, {tier=3, pc=pc, op=n})
end

local function bundle_call(bundles, n, op, pc)
  local callee = reg(op.a)
  make_bundle(bundles, "call:" .. n .. ":" .. pc, {
    ax_slot(callee, "is_closure", {role="callee"}),
    ax_slot(callee, "known_call_target", {role="callee"}),
    ax_slot(callee, "target_eq", {role="call_target", value=pc_id(pc)}),
  }, {tier=4, pc=pc, op=n})
end

function M.axes_for_ops(ops)
  local axes, seen = {}, {}
  local bundles = {}
  for pc, op in ipairs(ops or {}) do
    local n = opname(op)
    if BIN_RR[n] then bundle_numeric_rr(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif BIN_RI[n] then bundle_numeric_ri(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif BIN_K[n] then bundle_numeric_k(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif UNARY_I64[n] then bundle_unary(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif CMP_RR[n] then bundle_cmp_rr(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif CMP_RI[n] then bundle_cmp_ri(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif CMP_K[n] then bundle_cmp_k(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif FIELD_GET[n] then bundle_field_get(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif FIELD_SET[n] then bundle_field_set(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif ARRAY_GET[n] then bundle_array_get(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif ARRAY_SET[n] then bundle_array_set(bundles, n, op, pc)
    elseif CALL[n] then bundle_call(bundles, n, op, pc)
    end
  end

  for _, b in ipairs(bundles) do
    b.axis_refs = {}
    for _, ax in ipairs(b.axes or {}) do
      local ref = add_axis(axes, seen, ax)
      if ref then b.axis_refs[#b.axis_refs + 1] = ref end
    end
  end
  axes.bundles = bundles
  return axes
end

local function fact_for_axis(ax)
  if ax.global then
