          local off_src_t = a:mov_r8_m8("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 8))
          make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_t, "abs32s", sh, 8)
          local off_dst_t = a:mov_m8_r8("r10", 8, "rax")
          field_reloc(off_dst_t, 8)
        elseif src_loc.kind == "tvalue_regs" then
          local off_dst_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r10", 0, src_loc.value_reg)
          field_reloc(off_dst_v, 0)
          local off_dst_t = a:mov_m8_r8("r10", 8, src_loc.tag_reg)
          field_reloc(off_dst_t, 8)
        else
          errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native FieldStore input: " .. tostring(src_loc.kind)
        end
      elseif not src_loc then
        errors[#errors + 1] = "missing native FieldStore value"
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "ArrayLoad" then
      local tab_l = input_tvalue_loc(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local idx = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])
      local base_h = n.hole
      local out = n.outputs and n.outputs[1]
      local value_reg = alloc()
      local tag_reg = alloc()
      if tab_l and idx and base_h and value_reg and tag_reg then
        load_table_ptr_from_tvalue(tab_l)
        local off_base = a:mov_r64_m64("r10", "r10", 0)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_base, "abs32s", base_h, 0)
        a:dec_r64(idx)
        a:shift_r64_imm8("shl", idx, 4)
        a:mov_r64_m64_index(value_reg, "r10", idx, 1, 0)
        a:mov_r8_m8_index(tag_reg, "r10", idx, 1, 8)
        loc[out] = { kind = "tvalue_regs", value_reg = value_reg, tag_reg = tag_reg }
      end
      consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[1]); consume(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])

    elseif op == "ArrayStore" then
      local tab_l = input_tvalue_loc(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local idx = input_reg(n.inputs and n.inputs[2])
      local src_v = n.inputs and n.inputs[3]
      local src_loc = loc[src_v]
      local base_h = n.hole
      if tab_l and idx and src_loc and base_h then
        load_table_ptr_from_tvalue(tab_l)
        local off_base = a:mov_r64_m64("r10", "r10", 0)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_base, "abs32s", base_h, 0)
        a:dec_r64(idx)
        a:shift_r64_imm8("shl", idx, 4)
        if src_loc.kind == "slot" then
          local sh = src_loc.hole
          local off_src_v = a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 0)); make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_v, "abs32s", sh, 0)
          a:mov_m64_index_r64("r10", idx, 1, 0, "rax")
          local off_src_t = a:mov_r8_m8("rax", "r11", slot_default_disp(sh, 8)); make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_src_t, "abs32s", sh, 8)
          a:mov_m8_index_r8("r10", idx, 1, 8, "rax")
        elseif src_loc.kind == "tvalue_regs" then
          a:mov_m64_index_r64("r10", idx, 1, 0, src_loc.value_reg)
          a:mov_m8_index_r8("r10", idx, 1, 8, src_loc.tag_reg)
        else
          errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native ArrayStore input: " .. tostring(src_loc.kind)
        end
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "BarrierCheck" then
      local owner_l = input_tvalue_loc(n.inputs and n.inputs[1])
      local value_l = loc[n.inputs and n.inputs[2]]
      if owner_l and value_l then
        if owner_l.kind == "tvalue_regs" and owner_l.cell_hole then load_upvalue_cell(owner_l.cell_hole, "r10") else load_table_ptr_from_tvalue(owner_l) end
        a:mov_r64_r64("rsi", "r10")
        local vp = tvalue_address(value_l, "rdx")
        if not vp then
          -- Value already in registers; spill to scratch[0] so the primitive receives a stable TValue pointer.
          if value_l.kind == "tvalue_regs" then
            a:mov_m64_r64("rdi", 16, value_l.value_reg)
            a:mov_m8_r8("rdi", 24, value_l.tag_reg)
            a:lea_r64_base("rdx", "rdi", 16)
          else
            errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported BarrierCheck value: " .. tostring(value_l.kind)
          end
        end
        a:push_r64("rdi")
        a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "rdi", CTX_PRIMS)
        a:mov_r64_m64("rax", "rax", PRIM_BARRIER)
        a:call_r64("rax")
        a:pop_r64("rdi")
        a:mov_r64_m64("r11", "rdi", CTX_STACK)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "StoreSlot" then
      local input = n.inputs and n.inputs[1]
      store_tvalue(n.hole, loc[input], input)
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    elseif op == "StoreI64Slot" then
      local input = n.inputs and n.inputs[1]
      local src = input_reg(input)
      local h = n.hole
      if src and h then
        local off_v = a:mov_m64_r64("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 0), src)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_v, "abs32s", h, 0)
        local off_t = a:mov_m8_imm8("r11", slot_default_disp(h, 8), LUA_VNUMINT)
        make_data_reloc(data_relocs, off_t, "abs32s", h, 8)
        if #(n.outputs or {}) > 0 then
          for _, v in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do loc[v] = { kind = "boxed_i64_reg", reg = src } end
          -- Ownership of the register transfers from the raw I64 input to the
          -- boxed TValue output. Do not consume/free it here; the forwarded
          -- output will free it when its last consumer is emitted.
          loc[input] = nil
          counts[input] = 0
        else
          for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end
        end
      else
        for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end
      end

    elseif op == "ExitBoundary" then
      local label, ep_idx = ensure_stub(n)
      if label then
        local coff = a:jmp32(label)
        control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
          code_offset = coff,
          code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
          reloc_kind = "pc32",
          edge_kind = control_edge_kind(n),
          endpoint_index = ep_idx,
          source = n.source,
        })
        record_stub(label, n)
      end
      for _, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do consume(v) end

    else
      errors[#errors + 1] = "unsupported native x64 stencil op: " .. tostring(op)
    end
  end

  -- Success is an explicit patchable fallthrough edge. If a materializer does
  -- not patch it, the rel32 targets the local ok endpoint stub below.
  local ok_endpoint_index = opts.ok_endpoint_index or 2
  local ok_label = "ok_ep_" .. tostring(ok_endpoint_index)
  local ok_off = a:jmp32(ok_label)
  control_relocs[#control_relocs + 1] = StencilDesc.control_reloc({
    code_offset = ok_off,
    code_offset_kind = "x86_64_rel32",
    reloc_kind = "pc32",
    edge_kind = "fallthrough",
    endpoint_index = ok_endpoint_index,
    source = 0,
  })

  a:mark(ok_label)
  a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_KIND, SPON_EXIT_NONE)
  a:ret()

  local labels = {}
  for label in pairs(stubs) do labels[#labels + 1] = label end
  table.sort(labels)
  for _, label in ipairs(labels) do
    local n = stubs[label]
    local source = tonumber(n.source or 0) or 0
    a:mark(label)
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_KIND, op_exit_kind(n))
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_PC, source)
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX, math.max(0, source - 1))
    a:mov_m32_imm32("rdi", CTX_EXIT_HOLE, 0)
    a:ret()
  end

  for _, e in ipairs(errors) do a:error(e) end
  local bytes, ferrs = a:finish()
  if not bytes then return nil, ferrs end

  return {
    bytes = bytes,
    code_hex = bytes_to_hex(bytes),
    code_size = #bytes,
    data_relocs = data_relocs,
    control_relocs = control_relocs,
    layout = {
      mode = "native_bytes",
      executable = true,
      arch = "x86_64",
      encoding = "raw_function_body",
      entry_offset = 0,
      code_size = #bytes,
      fixed_width_patch_sites = true,
    },
  }
end

M.CTX_OFFSETS = {
  stack = CTX_STACK,
  exit_kind = CTX_EXIT_KIND,
  exit_pc = CTX_EXIT_PC,
  exit_op_idx = CTX_EXIT_OP_IDX,
  exit_hole = CTX_EXIT_HOLE,
}

return M

-- grammar_enum.lua — Complete PUC Lua bytecode grammar enumeration
--
-- Generates ALL grammatically-valid opcode sequences up to arity 4.
-- Every PUC Lua 5.4 opcode is included, grouped by operand layout.

local Util = require("src.util")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Facts = require("src.facts")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")

local M = {}

-- Slot tracker: register allocation for data-flow consistency
local function SlotTracker()
    return setmetatable({
        next_slot = 0, written = {}, read_only = {},
    }, { __index = {
        alloc = function(self, n)
            local s = self.next_slot
            self.next_slot = self.next_slot + (n or 1)
            self.written[tostring(s)] = true
            return s
        end,
        read = function(self, _)
            for s, _ in pairs(self.written) do return tonumber(s) end
            local s = self.next_slot
            self.next_slot = self.next_slot + 1
            self.read_only[tostring(s)] = true
            return s
        end,
        clone = function(self)
            local c = SlotTracker()
            c.next_slot = self.next_slot
            for k, v in pairs(self.written) do c.written[k] = v end
            for k, v in pairs(self.read_only) do c.read_only[k] = v end
            return c
        end,
    }})
end

-- Opcode categories — every PUC Lua 5.4 opcode, grouped by operand pattern.
--   name:   category label
--   reps:   concrete opcode names in this category
--   is_terminal: true for branch/jump/return
--   gen:    function(seq_id, pos, slots, rep_name) -> {op=..., a=..., b=..., c=...}

local CATEGORIES = {}

-- Helper: register write + one register read
local function cat1w1r(name, reps, gen_extra)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=false,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            local src = slots:read(1)
            local dst = slots:alloc(1)
            local ev = {op=r, a=dst, b=src}
            if gen_extra then gen_extra(ev, slots) end
            return ev
        end}
end

-- Helper: register write + two register reads
local function cat1w2r(name, reps)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=false,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            return {op=r, a=slots:alloc(1), b=slots:read(1), c=slots:read(1)}
        end}
end

-- Helper: register write only (immediate/constant)
local function cat1w(name, reps, gen_extra)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=false,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            local ev = {op=r, a=slots:alloc(1)}
            if gen_extra then gen_extra(ev, slots) end
            return ev
        end}
end

-- Helper: register write + one register read + immediate
local function cat1w1r_imm(name, reps)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=false,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            return {op=r, a=slots:alloc(1), b=slots:read(1), c=5}
        end}
end

-- Helper: register write + two register reads + immediate
local function cat1w2r_k(name, reps)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=false,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            return {op=r, a=slots:alloc(1), b=slots:read(1), c=slots:read(1), k=0}
        end}
end

-- Helper: table write (reg, key, value)
local function cat_table_set(name, reps)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=false,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            local tbl = slots:read(1)
            local key = slots:read(1)
            local val = slots:read(1)
            return {op=r, a=tbl, b=key, c=val}
        end}
end

-- Helper: CALL-like (not a terminator but has complex register effects)
local function cat_call(name, reps)
    CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES + 1] = {name=name, reps=reps, is_terminal=true,
        gen = function(_, _, slots, r)
            local fn = slots:read(1)
            return {op=r, a=fn, b=0, c=0}
        end}
end

-- ── Continuers (can appear at any position) ──────────────────────────

-- LOAD immediate
cat1w("LOADI",   {"LOADI"},   function(ev) ev.sbx = 42 end)
cat1w("LOADF",   {"LOADF"},   function(ev) ev.sbx = 42 end)
cat1w("LOADK",   {"LOADK"},   function(ev) ev.bx = 0 end)

-- LOAD boolean/nil
cat1w("LOADTRUE",  {"LOADTRUE","LFALSESKIP"})
cat1w("LOADFALSE", {"LOADFALSE"})
cat1w("LOADNIL",   {"LOADNIL"})

-- MOVE
cat1w1r("MOVE", {"MOVE"})

-- Upvalue load/store (reads/writes upvalues, not register-to-register)
cat1w("GETUPVAL",  {"GETUPVAL"}, function(ev,slots) ev.b = 0 end)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="SETUPVAL", reps={"SETUPVAL"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:read(1), b=0} end}

-- Binary ops: RA = RB op RC
cat1w2r("ADD", {"ADD","SUB","MUL","DIV","MOD","POW","IDIV"})
cat1w2r("BAND", {"BAND","BOR","BXOR","SHL","SHR"})

-- Binary ops with immediate: RA = RB op sC
cat1w1r_imm("ADDI", {"ADDI"})
cat1w1r_imm("SHLI", {"SHLI","SHRI"})

-- Binary ops with constant key: RA = RB op K[C]
cat1w2r_k("ADDK", {"ADDK","SUBK","MULK","MODK","POWK","DIVK","IDIVK"})
cat1w2r_k("BANDK", {"BANDK","BORK","BXORK"})

-- Unary: RA = op(RB)
cat1w1r("UNM", {"UNM","BNOT","NOT","LEN"})

-- Comparison: Lua 5.5 A B k format (no A_out, synthetic dest)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="EQ", reps={"EQ","LT","LE"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) 
        local ra = slots:read(1)  -- register A
        local rb = slots:read(1)  -- register B
        local res = slots:alloc(1) -- synthetic dest
        return {op=r, a=ra, b=rb, dest=res, k=0}
    end}

-- Comparison with constant key (Lua 5.5: A B k)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="EQK", reps={"EQK"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) 
        local ra = slots:read(1)  -- register A
        return {op=r, a=ra, bx=0, k=0}
    end}

-- Comparison with immediate (Lua 5.5: A sB k — A=reg, sB=imm, no A_out)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="EQI", reps={"EQI","LTI","LEI","GTI","GEI"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r)
        local ra = slots:read(1)  -- A is the register
        local res = slots:alloc(1) -- synthetic dest (comparison produces 0/1)
        return {op=r, a=ra, sb=5, dest=res, k=0}
    end}

-- TEST / TESTSET
cat1w1r("TEST",    {"TEST"},    function(ev,slots) ev.c = 0 end)
cat1w1r("TESTSET", {"TESTSET"}, function(ev,slots) ev.c = 0 end)

-- Table read: RA = table[RB]
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="GETTABLE", reps={"GETTABLE","GETI"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:alloc(1), b=slots:read(1), c=slots:read(1)} end}
cat1w1r("GETFIELD", {"GETFIELD","GETTABUP"})
cat1w1r("SELF", {"SELF"}, function(ev,slots) ev.c = slots:read(1) end)

-- Table write: RA[RB] = RC
cat_table_set("SETTABLE", {"SETTABLE","SETI"})
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="SETFIELD", reps={"SETFIELD"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:read(1), b=slots:read(1), c=0} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="SETTABUP", reps={"SETTABUP"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=0, b=slots:read(1), c=slots:read(1)} end}

-- NEWTABLE
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="NEWTABLE", reps={"NEWTABLE"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:alloc(1), b=0, c=0} end}

-- CONCAT: RA = concat(RB..RC)
cat1w2r("CONCAT", {"CONCAT"})

-- CLOSURE
cat1w("CLOSURE", {"CLOSURE"}, function(ev) ev.bx = 0 end)

-- VARARG
cat1w("VARARG", {"VARARG"}, function(ev,slots) ev.b = 2 end)

-- SETLIST
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="SETLIST", reps={"SETLIST"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:read(1), b=0, c=0} end}

-- MMBIN / MMBINI / MMBINK (metamethod binary operations)
cat1w2r("MMBIN",   {"MMBIN"})
cat1w1r_imm("MMBINI", {"MMBINI"})
cat1w("MMBINK", {"MMBINK"}, function(ev,slots) ev.b = slots:read(1) end)

-- CLOSE / TBC (close upvalues)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="CLOSE", reps={"CLOSE"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:read(1)} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="TBC", reps={"TBC"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) return {op=r, a=slots:read(1)} end}

-- LOADKX (extra large constant)
cat1w("LOADKX", {"LOADKX"}, function(ev) ev.ax = 0 end)

-- FORPREP / TFORPREP (branch, but target is reachable as continuer)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="FORPREP", reps={"FORPREP"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) local v=slots:read(1); return {op=r, a=v, sbx=3} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="TFORPREP", reps={"TFORPREP"}, is_terminal=false,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,r) local v=slots:read(1); return {op=r, a=v, sbx=3} end}

-- ── Terminators (last position only) ─────────────────────────────────

-- JMP (isJ format — 25-bit signed offset, not sBx!)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="JMP", reps={"JMP"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,_,_) return {op="JMP", sj=-1} end}

-- RETURN
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="RETURN0", reps={"RETURN0"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,_,_) return {op="RETURN0"} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="RETURN1", reps={"RETURN1"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,_) return {op="RETURN1", a=slots:read(1)} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="RETURN", reps={"RETURN"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,_) return {op="RETURN", a=slots:read(1)} end}

-- CALL / TAILCALL
cat_call("CALL",     {"CALL"})
cat_call("TAILCALL", {"TAILCALL"})

-- FORLOOP / TFORCALL / TFORLOOP (conditional branch)
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="FORLOOP", reps={"FORLOOP"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,_) return {op="FORLOOP", a=slots:read(1), sbx=-3} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="TFORCALL", reps={"TFORCALL"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,_) return {op="TFORCALL", a=slots:read(1)} end}
CATEGORIES[#CATEGORIES+1] = {name="TFORLOOP", reps={"TFORLOOP"}, is_terminal=true,
    gen = function(_,_,slots,_) return {op="TFORLOOP", a=slots:read(1), sbx=-3} end}

-- ── generation logic ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

function M.generate_all(max_arity)
    max_arity = max_arity or 4

    local continuers, terminators = {}, {}
    for _, cat in ipairs(CATEGORIES) do
        if cat.is_terminal then terminators[#terminators + 1] = cat
        else continuers[#continuers + 1] = cat end
    end

    local sequences = {}

    local function pick_rep(cat, idx)
        local reps = cat.reps or {cat.name}
        return reps[((idx - 1) % #reps) + 1]
    end

    local function enumerate_len(L)
        -- All-continuer sequences
        local function gen(pos, cur, slots, idx)
            if pos > L then
                sequences[#sequences + 1] = {ops = cur}
                return
            end
            for ci, cat in ipairs(continuers) do
                local r = pick_rep(cat, idx + ci + pos)
                local s2 = slots:clone()
                local ev = cat.gen(pos, pos, s2, r)
                local nv = {}
                for _, o in ipairs(cur) do nv[#nv + 1] = o end
                nv[#nv + 1] = ev
                gen(pos + 1, nv, s2, idx + ci)
            end
        end
        gen(1, {}, SlotTracker(), 0)

        -- Sequences ending in a terminator (only if L >= 2)
        if L >= 2 then
            local function gen_t(pos, cur, slots, idx)
                if pos >= L then
                    for ti, cat in ipairs(terminators) do
                        local r = pick_rep(cat, idx + ti + pos)
                        local s2 = slots:clone()
                        local ev = cat.gen(pos, pos, s2, r)
                        local nv = {}
                        for _, o in ipairs(cur) do nv[#nv + 1] = o end
                        nv[#nv + 1] = ev
                        sequences[#sequences + 1] = {ops = nv}
                    end
                    return
                end
                for ci, cat in ipairs(continuers) do
                    local r = pick_rep(cat, idx + ci + pos)
                    local s2 = slots:clone()
                    local ev = cat.gen(pos, pos, s2, r)
                    local nv = {}
                    for _, o in ipairs(cur) do nv[#nv + 1] = o end
                    nv[#nv + 1] = ev
                    gen_t(pos + 1, nv, s2, idx + ci)
                end
            end
            gen_t(1, {}, SlotTracker(), 0)
        end
    end

        LOADI="LOAD_IMM", LOADF="LOAD_IMM",
        LOADK="LOAD_TBL", LOADKX="LOAD_TBL",
        LOADTRUE="LOAD_BOOL", LOADFALSE="LOAD_BOOL", LFALSESKIP="LOAD_BOOL",
        LOADNIL="LOAD_NIL",
        MOVE="MOVE", GETUPVAL="MOVE",
        SETUPVAL="SETUPV",
        NEWTABLE="OTHR", CONCAT="OTHR", CLOSURE="OTHR", VARARG="OTHR", SETLIST="OTHR",
        CALL="CALL", TAILCALL="CALL",
        RETURN="RET", RETURN0="RET", RETURN1="RET",
        JMP="JMP",
        CLOSE="CLOSE", TBC="CLOSE",
        ADD="BINOP_RR", SUB="BINOP_RR", MUL="BINOP_RR", DIV="BINOP_RR", MOD="BINOP_RR",
        POW="BINOP_RR", IDIV="BINOP_RR",
        BAND="BINOP_RR", BOR="BINOP_RR", BXOR="BINOP_RR", SHL="BINOP_RR", SHR="BINOP_RR",
        ADDI="BINOP_RI", SHLI="BINOP_RI", SHRI="BINOP_RI",
        ADDK="BINOP_K", SUBK="BINOP_K", MULK="BINOP_K",
        MODK="BINOP_K", POWK="BINOP_K", DIVK="BINOP_K", IDIVK="BINOP_K",
        BANDK="BINOP_K", BORK="BINOP_K", BXORK="BINOP_K",
        UNM="UNARY", BNOT="UNARY", NOT="UNARY", LEN="UNARY",
        GETTABLE="TBL_GET", GETI="TBL_GET",
        GETFIELD="FLD_GET", GETTABUP="FLD_GET", SELF="FLD_GET",
        SETTABLE="TBL_SET", SETI="TBL_SET",
        SETFIELD="FLD_SET", SETTABUP="FLD_SET",
        EQ="CMP_RR", LT="CMP_RR", LE="CMP_RR",
        EQK="CMP_K",
        EQI="CMP_RI", LTI="CMP_RI", LEI="CMP_RI", GTI="CMP_RI", GEI="CMP_RI",
        TEST="TEST", TESTSET="TESTSET",
        MMBIN="MMBIN", MMBINI="MMBINI", MMBINK="MMBINK",
        FORPREP="FPREP", TFORPREP="FPREP",
        FORLOOP="FLOOP", TFORLOOP="FLOOP", TFORCALL="FLOOP",
    }
    local function handler_of(op)
        local n = type(op) == "table" and op.op or tostring(op)
        return handler_map[n] or "UNK"
    end

    local eq = {}
    for _, seq in ipairs(sequences) do
        local handlers = {}
        for _, op in ipairs(seq.ops) do
            handlers[#handlers + 1] = handler_of(op)
        end
        local key = table.concat(handlers, "|")
        if not eq[key] then
            eq[key] = {handlers = handlers, ops = seq.ops, count = 0}
        end
        eq[key].count = eq[key].count + 1
    end

    local deduped = {}
    for _, e in pairs(eq) do
        deduped[#deduped + 1] = {ops = e.ops, count = e.count, handlers = e.handlers}
    end

    return deduped
end

-- ── fact enumeration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

local function fact_axes_for_ops(ops)
    return FactAxes.axes_for_ops(ops)
end

local function fact_subsets(axes, config)
    return FactAxes.subsets(axes, config)
end

-- ── compile all ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function M.enumerate_grammar(config)
    config = config or {}
    local max_arity = tonumber(config.max_arity or 4) or 4

    local sequences = M.generate_all(max_arity)
    print(string.format("[grammar] Generated %d opcode sequences (arity 1-%d)", #sequences, max_arity))

    local forms_by_key, total_compiles, total_ok = {}, 0, 0

    for si, seq in ipairs(sequences) do
        local ops = seq.ops
        local axes = fact_axes_for_ops(ops)
        local subsets = fact_subsets(axes, config)

        for _, facts in ipairs(subsets) do
            local result = SSA.compile(ops, facts, config)
            total_compiles = total_compiles + 1
            if result.ok then
                total_ok = total_ok + 1
                local contract = Contract.from_result(result, facts)
                local key = result.stencil_hash
                if not forms_by_key[key] then
                    forms_by_key[key] = {
                        key = result.stencil_hash, dedupe_key = key, stencil_hash = result.stencil_hash,
                        stencil_form = result.stencil_form,
                        stencil_ops = result.stencil_ops,
                        stencil_slotmaps = result.slotmaps,
                        ops = ops, facts = facts, contract = contract,
                        count = 0, changed = result.changed, source_ops = ops,
                    }
                end
                forms_by_key[key].count = forms_by_key[key].count + 1
            end
        end
        if si % 1000 == 0 then
            io.stderr:write(string.format("[grammar] %d/%d seq (x%d facts avg, %d OK, %d unique)...\n",
                si, #sequences, total_compiles > 0 and math.floor(total_compiles / si) or 0, total_ok, #forms_by_key))
        end
    end

    local unique_count = 0
    for _ in pairs(forms_by_key) do unique_count = unique_count + 1 end

    local stats = {sequences = #sequences, compiles = total_compiles, ok = total_ok, unique_forms = unique_count}

    local forms = {}
    for _, f in pairs(forms_by_key) do forms[#forms + 1] = f end
    table.sort(forms, function(a, b) return a.count > b.count end)

    print(string.format("[grammar] %d unique Stencil IR forms from %d compiles (%d OK, %d failed)",
        #forms, total_compiles, total_ok, total_compiles - total_ok))

    return {forms = forms, forms_by_key = forms_by_key, stats = stats}
end

-- Exact single-op floor set. This intentionally bypasses handler-class
-- deduplication so the runtime selector has a legal L0 fallback for every
-- concrete PUC opcode, not just one representative per handler class.
function M.generate_l0_all()
    local out, seen = {}, {}
    local function add(ev)
        if ev and ev.op and not seen[ev.op] then
            out[#out + 1] = {ops = {ev}, count = 1, l0 = true}
            seen[ev.op] = true
        end
    end
    for _, cat in ipairs(CATEGORIES) do
        for _, rep in ipairs(cat.reps or {}) do
            local slots = SlotTracker()
            local ev = cat.gen(1, 1, slots, rep)
            if rep == "GETTABLE" then ev.a, ev.b, ev.c = 2, 0, 1 end
            if rep == "POW" then ev.a, ev.b, ev.c = 2, 0, 1 end
            add(ev)
        end
    end
    -- Opcodes not otherwise represented in the reduced foundry grammar still
    -- need exact L0 floor stencils so runtime fallback is total over PUC Lua.
    add({op="GETVARG", a=0, b=0, c=0})
    add({op="ERRNNIL", a=0, bx=0})
    add({op="VARARGPREP"})
    add({op="EXTRAARG", ax=0})
    return out
end

-- Concrete hot L1 floors. Handler-class grammar is intentionally compact and
-- does not enumerate every operand shape. Runtime projection, however, needs
-- concrete aliases for common accumulator spans, otherwise a real bytecode run
-- like `x = x + 1` repeated can only select L0 one-op stencils. These floors
-- still go through SSA -> normal form -> dedupe -> native lowering; they only
-- seed concrete source aliases that the compact grammar omitted.
function M.generate_l1_fusion_floors(max_arity)
    max_arity = tonumber(max_arity or 4) or 4
    local out = {}
    local function clone_ops(ops)
        local r = {}
        for i, op in ipairs(ops) do
            local c = {}
            for k, v in pairs(op) do c[k] = v end
            r[i] = c
        end
        return r
    end
    local function add(ops)
        if #ops >= 2 and #ops <= max_arity then
            out[#out + 1] = {ops = clone_ops(ops), count = 1, l1_floor = true}
        end
    end

    -- Accumulator immediate-add chains match PUC output for repeated
    -- `x = x + imm`: A and B are the same register; C is a patchable sC
    -- placeholder and the L1 materializer supplies the real bytecode immediate.
    for len = 2, math.min(max_arity, 4) do
        local ops = {}
        for i = 1, len do ops[i] = {op="ADDI", a=0, b=0, c=5} end
        add(ops)
    end

    -- Register/register accumulator floors. The compact handler grammar's L0
    -- representative often uses the same logical source for B/C; real bytecode
    -- commonly uses an accumulator plus a stable operand register.
    for len = 2, math.min(max_arity, 4) do
        local ops = {}
        for i = 1, len do ops[i] = {op = (i % 2 == 1) and "ADD" or "SUB", a=0, b=0, c=1} end
        add(ops)
    end

    -- Constant-key arithmetic accumulator floors. These exercise RK/constant
    -- patching and the const_i64 selector fact without resorting to fake
    -- benchmark-only native code.
    for len = 2, math.min(max_arity, 4) do
        local ops = {}
        for i = 1, len do ops[i] = {op = (i % 2 == 1) and "ADDK" or "SUBK", a=0, b=0, c=0, k=0} end
        add(ops)
    end

    -- Mixed hot straight-line integer shape: immediate + RR + RR + K. This is
    -- intentionally concrete because the runtime selector keys by concrete
    -- opcode chain while normal-form dedupe still merges semantic equivalents.
    if max_arity >= 4 then
        add({
            {op="ADDI", a=0, b=0, c=5},
            {op="ADD",  a=0, b=0, c=1},
            {op="SUB",  a=0, b=0, c=1},
            {op="ADDK", a=0, b=0, c=0, k=0},
        })
    end

    -- Common straight-line prologue: initialize an accumulator, then mutate it.
    for len = 2, math.min(max_arity, 4) do
        local ops = {{op="LOADI", a=0, sbx=42}}
        for i = 2, len do ops[i] = {op="ADDI", a=0, b=0, c=5} end
        add(ops)
    end

    return out
end

return M

-- worker_compile.lua — One parallel worker: SSA compile + stencil normal forms.
-- Usage: luajit src/worker_compile.lua <chunk_id>
--
-- Workers run the foundry semantic pass only:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA -> canonical normal form.
-- They do not lower descriptors. Global unique final forms are merged and lowered
-- once by dedupe_normal_forms.lua before C bank generation.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")
local Util = require("src.util")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local ci = tonumber(arg[1])
assert(ci, "usage: luajit src/worker_compile.lua <chunk_id>")

local config = {
  max_fact_combos = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_FACT_COMBOS") or "0") or 0,
  fact_axis_mode = tostring(os.getenv("FACT_AXIS_MODE") or "curated"),
}
local progress_every = tonumber(os.getenv("WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS") or "1000") or 1000

local tmpdir = os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "/tmp"
local function tmp(name) return tmpdir .. "/" .. name end
local seqs = Util.read_json(tmp("grammar_chunk_" .. ci .. ".json"))

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] START seqs=%d fact_mode=%s max_fact_combos=%s\n",
  ci, #seqs, config.fact_axis_mode, tostring(config.max_fact_combos)))

local function op_signature(ops)
  -- Pattern aliases must preserve exact concrete L0 operand shapes. Opcode-only
  -- signatures collapse single-op L0 floor aliases into multi-op normal-form
  -- representatives and leave spon_l0 without a concrete exact entry.
  return cjson.encode(ops or {})
end

local function contract_key(contract)
  return table.concat({
    tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.required_sig and contract.required_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.checked_sig and contract.checked_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.produced_sig and contract.produced_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.killed_sig and contract.killed_sig.literal or ""),
  }, "|")
end

local db_path = tmp("grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite")
os.remove(db_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(db_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE forms (
  dedupe_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE ssa_rejections (
  reason TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_form = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))
local bump_form = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE forms SET count = count + 1 WHERE dedupe_key = ?]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))

local function step_done(stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local forms_seen = {}
local rejection_counts = {}
local lc, lssa_ok, nforms = 0, 0, 0

for si, ops in ipairs(seqs) do
  local subsets = FactAxes.subsets(FactAxes.axes_for_ops(ops), config)
  for _, facts in ipairs(subsets) do
    local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts, config)
    lc = lc + 1
    if r.ok then
      lssa_ok = lssa_ok + 1
      local contract = Contract.from_result(r, facts)
      local ckey = contract_key(contract)
      local normal_key = tostring(r.stencil_hash)
      -- Equivalence is the SSA-consumed canonical normal form. The contract is
      -- retained as descriptor metadata for the chosen representative, but it is
      -- not part of the dedupe key: source facts/opcodes are aliases of the form.
      local dedupe_key = normal_key
      local ops_key = op_signature(ops)

      insert_pattern:bind_values(dedupe_key, ops_key, cjson.encode(ops), 1)
      step_done(insert_pattern)

      if forms_seen[dedupe_key] then
        bump_form:bind_values(dedupe_key)
        step_done(bump_form)
      else
        nforms = nforms + 1
        forms_seen[dedupe_key] = true
        local form = {
          key = normal_key,
          dedupe_key = dedupe_key,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_form = r.stencil_form,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          stencil_slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
          contract = contract,
          count = 1,
          changed = r.changed,
          source_ops = ops,
        }
        local ssa_payload = {
          stencil = r.stencil,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          active_node_specs = r.active_node_specs,
          checked_facts = r.checked_facts,
          deps = r.deps,
          projection = r.projection,
          source_ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
        }
        local selector_sig = tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or "0x0000000000000000ULL")
        insert_form:bind_values(dedupe_key, normal_key, ckey, selector_sig, 1, cjson.encode(form), cjson.encode(ssa_payload))
        step_done(insert_form)
      end
    else
      if #(r.rejections or {}) > 0 then
        for _, rej in ipairs(r.rejections or {}) do
          local reason = tostring(rej.reason or "compile_rejected")
          rejection_counts[reason] = (rejection_counts[reason] or 0) + 1
        end
      elseif #(r.errors or {}) > 0 then
        for _, err in ipairs(r.errors or {}) do
          local reason = tostring(err):gsub("%s+", "_"):sub(1, 120)
          rejection_counts[reason] = (rejection_counts[reason] or 0) + 1
        end
      else
        rejection_counts.compile_rejected = (rejection_counts.compile_rejected or 0) + 1
      end
    end
  end
  if progress_every > 0 and (si % progress_every == 0 or si == #seqs) then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] %d/%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d unique_forms=%d\n", ci, si, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, nforms))
  end
end

insert_form:finalize()
bump_form:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
local stats = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)]]))
local ssa_rejected = lc - lssa_ok
for _, kv in ipairs({
  {"seqs", #seqs},
  {"compiles", lc},
  {"ok", lssa_ok},
  {"ssa_ok", lssa_ok},
  {"rejected", ssa_rejected},
  {"forms", nforms},
  {"stencils", 0},
}) do
  stats:bind_values(kv[1], kv[2])
  step_done(stats)
end
stats:finalize()
local rej_stmt = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO ssa_rejections(reason, count) VALUES (?, ?)]]))
local rej_keys = {}
for k in pairs(rejection_counts) do rej_keys[#rej_keys + 1] = k end
 table.sort(rej_keys)
for _, k in ipairs(rej_keys) do
  rej_stmt:bind_values(k, rejection_counts[k])
  step_done(rej_stmt)
end
rej_stmt:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT; CREATE INDEX forms_stencil_hash_idx ON forms(stencil_hash); CREATE INDEX patterns_ops_key_idx ON patterns(ops_key);") == sqlite3.OK)
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] DONE seqs=%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d rejected=%d unique_forms=%d db=%s\n", ci, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, ssa_rejected, nforms, db_path))

-- dedupe_normal_forms.lua -- Global foundry normal-form merge + unique lowering.
--
-- This is the semantic boundary that the monolithic foundry implies:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA consumes semantics -> canonical normal form
--   -> global equivalence class -> lower one representative to the descriptor bank.
--
-- Per-worker DBs contain normal forms and pattern aliases. This script merges
-- equivalent forms across workers, lowers each unique final form once, and keeps
-- all source-pattern aliases so the C selector can map many opcode sequences to
-- the same unique bank id.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local Util = require("src.util")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")
local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")

local tmpdir = arg[1] or os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "build/stencil_bank"
local chunks = tonumber(arg[2] or os.getenv("CHUNKS") or "128") or 128
local out_path = arg[3] or (tmpdir .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite")

local function worker_path(ci) return tmpdir .. "/grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite" end

local function step_done_for(db, stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local function numeric_key_map(t)
  if type(t) ~= "table" then return t end
  local out = {}
  for k, v in pairs(t) do out[tonumber(k) or k] = v end
  return out
end

local function rehydrate_ssa_payload(p)
  p = p or {}
  local st = p.stencil or {}
  st.slot_class_by_concrete = numeric_key_map(st.slot_class_by_concrete)
  st.slot_concrete_by_class = st.slot_concrete_by_class or {}
  p.stencil = st
  return p
end

os.remove(out_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(out_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE unique_forms (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  first_chunk INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  lower_ok INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  bank_id INTEGER UNIQUE,
  stencil_json TEXT,
  lower_errors_json TEXT
);
CREATE TABLE form_patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE chunk_stats (
  chunk INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  seqs INTEGER NOT NULL,
  compiles INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ssa_ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  rejected INTEGER NOT NULL,
  forms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  stencils INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE bank_stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE ssa_rejections (
  reason TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_unique = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO unique_forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, first_chunk, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO form_patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_chunk = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO chunk_stats
  (chunk, seqs, compiles, ok, ssa_ok, rejected, forms, stencils)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))
local insert_rejection = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO ssa_rejections(reason, count) VALUES (?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(reason) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))

local totals = {seqs = 0, compiles = 0, ok = 0, ssa_ok = 0, rejected = 0, forms = 0, stencils = 0, patterns = 0}

for ci = 1, chunks do
  local path = worker_path(ci)
  assert(Util.path_exists(path), "missing worker db: " .. path)
  local w = assert(sqlite3.open(path))
  local stats = {}
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT key, value FROM stats") do stats[row.key] = tonumber(row.value) or 0 end
  insert_chunk:bind_values(ci, stats.seqs or 0, stats.compiles or 0, stats.ok or 0, stats.ssa_ok or 0, stats.rejected or 0, stats.forms or 0, stats.stencils or 0)
  step_done_for(db, insert_chunk)
  totals.seqs = totals.seqs + (stats.seqs or 0)
  totals.compiles = totals.compiles + (stats.compiles or 0)
  totals.ok = totals.ok + (stats.ok or 0)
  totals.ssa_ok = totals.ssa_ok + (stats.ssa_ok or 0)
  totals.rejected = totals.rejected + (stats.rejected or 0)
  totals.forms = totals.forms + (stats.forms or 0)
  totals.stencils = totals.stencils + (stats.stencils or 0)

  local n = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json FROM forms ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_unique:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.stencil_hash, row.contract_key, row.selector_sig, tonumber(row.count) or 1, ci, row.form_json, row.ssa_json)
    step_done_for(db, insert_unique)
    n = n + 1
    if n % 50000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d merged %d forms\n", ci, n)) end
  end

  for row in w:nrows("SELECT reason, count FROM ssa_rejections ORDER BY reason") do
    insert_rejection:bind_values(row.reason, tonumber(row.count) or 0)
    step_done_for(db, insert_rejection)
  end

  local pn = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count FROM patterns ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_pattern:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.ops_key, row.ops_json, tonumber(row.count) or 1)
    step_done_for(db, insert_pattern)
    pn = pn + 1
  end
  totals.patterns = totals.patterns + pn
  w:close()
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d/%d forms=%d patterns=%d compiles=%d\n", ci, chunks, stats.forms or 0, pn, stats.compiles or 0))
end

insert_unique:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
insert_chunk:finalize()
insert_rejection:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

CREATE INDEX unique_forms_stencil_hash_idx ON unique_forms(stencil_hash);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_count_idx ON unique_forms(count);

local update_lower = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE unique_forms
  SET lower_ok = ?, bank_id = ?, stencil_json = ?, lower_errors_json = ? WHERE id = ?]]))
local unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected = 0, 0, 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT id, form_json, ssa_json FROM unique_forms ORDER BY id") do
  unique_forms = unique_forms + 1
  local id = tonumber(row.id)
  local form = assert(cjson.decode(row.form_json))
  local ssa = rehydrate_ssa_payload(assert(cjson.decode(row.ssa_json)))
  local lower = StencilLower.generate(ssa, { facts = form.facts or ssa.facts or {} })
  if lower.ok then
    local stencil = lower.stencil
    if stencil.exact ~= true then error("lowered non-exact stencil for unique form " .. tostring(id)) end
    lowered_stencils = lowered_stencils + 1
    stencil.stencil_id = lowered_stencils
    stencil.abi = StencilDesc.lower_to_abi(stencil)
    update_lower:bind_values(1, lowered_stencils, cjson.encode(stencil), nil, id)
  else
    update_lower:bind_values(0, nil, nil, cjson.encode(lower.errors or {}), id)
    lower_rejected = lower_rejected + 1
  end
  step_done_for(db, update_lower)
  if unique_forms % 50000 == 0 then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] lowered %d unique forms, stencils=%d rejected=%d\n", unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected))
  end
end
update_lower:finalize()

for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM form_patterns") do pattern_aliases = tonumber(row.n) or 0 end

local function stat(k, v)

  step_done_for(db, stat_stmt)
end

-- puc_bytecode.lua
-- Static PUC Lua bytecode operand/liveness-ish fact extraction.

local M = {}
local util = require("src.util")
local FactSchema = require("src.fact_schema")
local LoopRegions = require("src.loop_regions")

local function find_lua_root(repo_root, awfy_root)
    local candidates = {
        awfy_root,
        repo_root and (repo_root .. "/.vendor/Lua"),
        ".vendor/Lua",
    }
    for _, p in ipairs(candidates) do
        if p and util.path_exists(p .. "/liblua.a") and util.path_exists(p .. "/lopcodes.h") then
            return p
        end
    end
    return nil
end

local function build_tool(config, name, source_file)
    config = config or {}
    local repo_root = util.abspath(config.repo_root or util.find_repo_root(".") or ".")
    local lua_root = util.abspath(find_lua_root(repo_root, config.lua_root or config.awfy_root) or (repo_root .. "/.vendor/Lua"))
    local out = config[name .. "_path"] or config.tool_path or (repo_root .. "/experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/build/tools/" .. name)
    if util.path_exists(out) then return out end
    util.mkdir_p(util.dirname(out))
    local src = repo_root .. "/experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/tools/jit_harness/" .. source_file
    local cmd = string.format("cc -I%s %s %s -lm -ldl -o %s",
        util.shell_quote(lua_root), util.shell_quote(src), util.shell_quote(lua_root .. "/liblua.a"), util.shell_quote(out))
    local ok, text = util.run_capture(cmd)
    if not ok then return nil, text end
    return out
end

function M.ensure_dump_tool(config)
    return build_tool(config, "puc_proto_dump", "puc_proto_dump.c")
end

function M.ensure_trace_tool(config)
    return build_tool(config, "puc_trace_operands", "puc_trace_operands.c")
end

local function read_dump(path)
    local rows = {}
    local text = util.read_file(path)
    if not text then return rows end
    local header = true
    for line in text:gmatch("[^\n]+") do
        if header then header = false else
            local c = {}
            for col in (line .. "\t"):gmatch("([^\t]*)\t") do c[#c+1] = col end
            if #c >= 13 then
                rows[#rows+1] = {
                    proto = c[1], depth = tonumber(c[2]), pc = tonumber(c[3]), opcode = tonumber(c[4]), name = c[5],
                    a = tonumber(c[6]), b = tonumber(c[7]), c = tonumber(c[8]), k = tonumber(c[9]),
                    bx = tonumber(c[10]), sbx = tonumber(c[11]), ax = tonumber(c[12]), word = tonumber(c[13]),
                }
            end
        end
    end
    return rows
end

function M.dump_file(path, config)
    local tool, err = M.ensure_dump_tool(config)
    if not tool then return nil, err end
    local cmd = util.shell_quote(tool) .. " " .. util.shell_quote(path)
    local ok, out = util.run_capture(cmd)
    if not ok then return nil, out end
    local tmp = os.tmpname()
    util.write_file(tmp, out)
    local rows = read_dump(tmp)
    os.remove(tmp)
    return rows
end

local function op_key(a, b)
    return a.name .. "|" .. b.name
end

local function ins_event(row)
    return {
        op = row.name, name = row.name, proto = row.proto, pc = row.pc,
        opcode = row.opcode, a = row.a, b = row.b, c = row.c, k = row.k,
        bx = row.bx, sbx = row.sbx, ax = row.ax, word = row.word,
    }
end

local function ins_key(row)
    return string.format("%s(a=%s,b=%s,c=%s,k=%s,bx=%s,sbx=%s,ax=%s)",
        tostring(row.name), tostring(row.a), tostring(row.b), tostring(row.c), tostring(row.k),
        tostring(row.bx), tostring(row.sbx), tostring(row.ax))
end

local function operand_window_key(rows, i, j)
    local parts = {}
    for k = i, j do parts[#parts + 1] = ins_key(rows[k]) end
    return table.concat(parts, "|")
end

local function add_operand_window(result, rows, i, j, count)
    local key = operand_window_key(rows, i, j)
    local e = result.static_operand_windows[key]
    if not e then
        e = { key = key, count = 0, events = {} }
        for k = i, j do e.events[#e.events + 1] = ins_event(rows[k]) end
        result.static_operand_windows[key] = e
    end
    e.count = e.count + (count or 1)
end

local function fact_key_for_rewrite(kind, ops)
    if kind == "move_move_forward" then
        return "MOVE:move_def;MOVE:move_uses_previous_def"
    elseif kind == "move_move_empty" then
        return "MOVE:redundant_move;MOVE:redundant_move"
    elseif kind == "load_move_final_dst" then
        return ops[1] .. ":load_def;MOVE:move_uses_previous_def"
    elseif kind == "op_move_final_dst" then
        return ops[1] .. ":i64;MOVE:move_uses_previous_def"
    elseif kind == "op_return1" then
        return ops[1] .. ":i64;RETURN1:returns_previous_def"
    end
end

local pure_arith = { ADD=true, SUB=true, MUL=true, ADDI=true }
local pure_load = { LOADI=true, LOADF=true, LOADK=true }

local function classify_pair(a, b)
    local ops = { a.name, b.name }
    if a.name == "MOVE" and b.name == "MOVE" then
        if a.a == a.b and b.a == b.b then return "move_move_empty", ops end
        if a.a == b.b then return "move_move_forward", ops end
    end
    if pure_load[a.name] and b.name == "MOVE" and a.a == b.b then
        return "load_move_final_dst", ops
    end
    if pure_arith[a.name] and b.name == "MOVE" and a.a == b.b then
        return "op_move_final_dst", ops
    end
    if pure_arith[a.name] and b.name == "RETURN1" and a.a == b.a then
        return "op_return1", ops
    end
    return nil, ops
end

local function parse_rows_text(text)
    local rows = {}
    if not text then return rows end
    local header = true
    for line in text:gmatch("[^\n]+") do
        if header then header = false else
            local c = {}
            for col in (line .. "\t"):gmatch("([^\t]*)\t") do c[#c+1] = col end
            if #c >= 13 then
                rows[#rows+1] = {
                    seq = tonumber(c[1]), proto = c[2], pc = tonumber(c[3]), opcode = tonumber(c[4]), name = c[5],
                    a = tonumber(c[6]), b = tonumber(c[7]), c = tonumber(c[8]), k = tonumber(c[9]),
                    bx = tonumber(c[10]), sbx = tonumber(c[11]), ax = tonumber(c[12]), word = tonumber(c[13]),
                }
            end
        end
    end
    return rows
end

function M.trace_file(path, output_path, config)
    config = config or {}
    local tool, err = M.ensure_trace_tool(config)
    if not tool then return nil, err end
    local limit = tonumber(config.trace_limit or 100000) or 100000
    if output_path then util.mkdir_p(util.dirname(output_path)) end
    local parts = { util.shell_quote(tool), util.shell_quote(path), tostring(limit) }
    if output_path then parts[#parts + 1] = util.shell_quote(output_path) end
    local cmd = table.concat(parts, " ")
    if config.timeout_seconds then
        cmd = "timeout " .. tostring(tonumber(config.timeout_seconds) or 5) .. "s " .. cmd
    end
    if output_path == nil then
        local ok, out = util.run_capture(cmd)
        local rows = parse_rows_text(out)
        if #rows > 0 then return rows, out end
        if not ok then return nil, out end
        return rows, out
    end
    local ok, out = util.run_capture(cmd)
    if util.path_exists(output_path) then
        local rows = parse_rows_text(util.read_file(output_path))
        if #rows > 0 then return rows, out end
    end
    if not ok then return nil, out end
    return {}, out
end

local function accumulate_rewrite_facts_from_rows(rows, result, use_dynamic)
    for i = 1, #rows - 1 do
        local a, b = rows[i], rows[i + 1]
        if a.proto == b.proto and a.pc + 1 == b.pc then
            local key = op_key(a, b)
            if use_dynamic then
                result.dynamic_window_counts[key] = (result.dynamic_window_counts[key] or 0) + 1
            else
                result.static_window_counts[key] = (result.static_window_counts[key] or 0) + 1
            end
            local rkind, ops = classify_pair(a, b)
            if rkind then
                local fk = fact_key_for_rewrite(rkind, ops)
                local full = key .. " @ " .. fk
                local target = use_dynamic and result.rewrite_fact_counts or result.rewrite_fact_static_counts
                target[full] = (target[full] or 0) + 1
                result.operand_shape_counts[full] = (result.operand_shape_counts[full] or 0) + 1
            end
        end
    end
end

function M.profile_dynamic_files(files, config)
    config = config or {}
    local result = {
        files = 0,
        instructions = 0,
        dynamic_window_counts = {},
        operand_shape_counts = {},
        rewrite_fact_counts = {},
        rejects = {},
        trace_dir = config.trace_dir,
    }
    if config.trace_dir then util.mkdir_p(config.trace_dir) end
    local processed = 0
    local include = config.include
    print(string.format("[puc-dyn] tracing operands for up to %d/%d files", tonumber(config.max_files or #(files or {})), #(files or {})))
    for idx, file in ipairs(files or {}) do
        if config.max_files and processed >= config.max_files then break end
        local path = file.path or file
        if include and not tostring(path):find(include, 1, true) then
            -- skip
        else
            print(string.format("[puc-dyn] %d/%d %s", idx, #(files or {}), tostring(path)))
            local out = config.trace_dir and (config.trace_dir .. "/" .. util.basename(path):gsub("%.lua$", "") .. ".trace_operands.tsv") or nil
            local rows, err = M.trace_file(path, out, config)
            if not rows then
                print("[puc-dyn] reject: " .. tostring(err):sub(1, 160))
                result.rejects[#result.rejects+1] = { path = path, error = err }
            else
                processed = processed + 1
                result.files = result.files + 1
                result.instructions = result.instructions + #rows
                print(string.format("[puc-dyn] traced %d instructions", #rows))
                accumulate_rewrite_facts_from_rows(rows, result, true)
            end
        end
    end
    return result
end

function M.profile_files(files, config, dynamic_profile)

-- loop_regions.lua -- structural loop-region recognition for SpongeJIT.
--
-- Loop bytecodes are not scalar stencil atoms. This module recognizes validated
-- PUC-style loop topology as data so later region lowering can consume whole
-- loops with explicit edges, slot windows, and per-instance PC bindings.

local M = {}

M.DIALECT = "puc_lua_5_5"

local NUMERIC_FOR = { FORPREP = true, FORLOOP = true }
local GENERIC_FOR = { TFORPREP = true, TFORCALL = true, TFORLOOP = true }

function M.is_numeric_for_opcode(op) return NUMERIC_FOR[tostring(op or "")] == true end
function M.is_generic_for_opcode(op) return GENERIC_FOR[tostring(op or "")] == true end
function M.is_for_opcode(op) return M.is_numeric_for_opcode(op) or M.is_generic_for_opcode(op) end

local function name(row) return tostring(row and (row.name or row.op) or "") end
local function n(row, key) return tonumber(row and row[key]) end
local function proto_of(row) return row and row.proto or "" end

local function slot_window(first_slot, last_slot)
  local out = {}
  first_slot = tonumber(first_slot) or 0
  last_slot = tonumber(last_slot) or first_slot
  for s = first_slot, last_slot do out[#out + 1] = s end
  return out
end

local function reject(out, row, reason, detail)
  out[#out + 1] = {
    dialect = M.DIALECT,
    proto = proto_of(row),
    pc = n(row, "pc") or 0,
    op = name(row),
    reason = reason,
    detail = detail,
  }
end

local function row_index(rows)
  local by_pc = {}
  for _, row in ipairs(rows or {}) do by_pc[n(row, "pc") or -1] = row end
  return by_pc
end

local function numeric_region(row, by_pc, rejects)
  local prep_pc, prep_bx, base = n(row, "pc"), n(row, "bx"), n(row, "a")
  if prep_pc == nil or prep_bx == nil or base == nil then
    reject(rejects, row, "numeric_for_missing_fields")
    return nil
  end

  -- PUC parser encodes FORPREP.Bx as loop_pc - (prep_pc + 1).
  local loop_pc = prep_pc + 1 + prep_bx
  local loop = by_pc[loop_pc]
  if name(loop) ~= "FORLOOP" then
    reject(rejects, row, "numeric_for_missing_forloop", { expected_pc = loop_pc, found = name(loop) })
    return nil
  end
  if n(loop, "a") ~= base then
    reject(rejects, row, "numeric_for_base_mismatch", { loop_pc = loop_pc, prep_a = base, loop_a = n(loop, "a") })
    return nil
  end
  local expected_loop_bx = loop_pc - prep_pc
  if n(loop, "bx") ~= expected_loop_bx then
    reject(rejects, row, "numeric_for_backedge_mismatch", { loop_pc = loop_pc, expected_bx = expected_loop_bx, loop_bx = n(loop, "bx") })
    return nil
  end

  return {
    kind = "numeric_for_region",
    dialect = M.DIALECT,
    proto = proto_of(row),
    base = base,
    prep_pc = prep_pc,
    body_entry_pc = prep_pc + 1,
    loop_pc = loop_pc,
    exit_pc = loop_pc + 1,
    prep_bx = prep_bx,
    loop_bx = expected_loop_bx,
    slot_window = slot_window(base, base + 3),
    state_slots = {
      pre_prep = { init = base, limit = base + 1, step = base + 2, external = base + 3 },
      post_prep = { counter = base, step = base + 1, control = base + 2, external = base + 3 },
    },
    edges = {
      enter_body = { from_pc = prep_pc, to_pc = prep_pc + 1 },
      skip = { from_pc = prep_pc, to_pc = loop_pc + 1 },
      continue_loop = { from_pc = loop_pc, to_pc = prep_pc + 1 },
      done = { from_pc = loop_pc, to_pc = loop_pc + 1 },
    },
  }
end

local function generic_region(row, by_pc, rejects)
  local prep_pc, prep_bx, base = n(row, "pc"), n(row, "bx"), n(row, "a")
  if prep_pc == nil or prep_bx == nil or base == nil then
    reject(rejects, row, "generic_for_missing_fields")
    return nil
  end

  -- PUC TFORPREP jumps from pre-incremented PC to TFORCALL.
  local call_pc = prep_pc + 1 + prep_bx
  local call = by_pc[call_pc]
  if name(call) ~= "TFORCALL" then
    reject(rejects, row, "generic_for_missing_tforcall", { expected_pc = call_pc, found = name(call) })
    return nil
  end
  if n(call, "a") ~= base then
    reject(rejects, row, "generic_for_call_base_mismatch", { call_pc = call_pc, prep_a = base, call_a = n(call, "a") })
    return nil
  end

  local loop_pc = call_pc + 1
  local loop = by_pc[loop_pc]
  if name(loop) ~= "TFORLOOP" then
    reject(rejects, row, "generic_for_missing_tforloop", { expected_pc = loop_pc, found = name(loop) })
    return nil
  end
  if n(loop, "a") ~= base then
    reject(rejects, row, "generic_for_loop_base_mismatch", { loop_pc = loop_pc, prep_a = base, loop_a = n(loop, "a") })
    return nil
  end
  local expected_loop_bx = loop_pc - prep_pc
  if n(loop, "bx") ~= expected_loop_bx then
    reject(rejects, row, "generic_for_backedge_mismatch", { loop_pc = loop_pc, expected_bx = expected_loop_bx, loop_bx = n(loop, "bx") })
    return nil
  end

  local nresults = n(call, "c") or 0
  local last_slot = base + math.max(5, 3 + nresults)
  return {
    kind = "generic_for_region",
    dialect = M.DIALECT,
    proto = proto_of(row),
    base = base,
    prep_pc = prep_pc,
    body_entry_pc = prep_pc + 1,
    call_pc = call_pc,
    loop_pc = loop_pc,
    exit_pc = loop_pc + 1,
    prep_bx = prep_bx,
    loop_bx = expected_loop_bx,
    result_count = nresults,
    slot_window = slot_window(base, last_slot),
    state_slots = {
      function_slot = base,
      state_slot = base + 1,
      closing_slot = base + 2,
      control_slot = base + 3,
      call_func_slot = base + 3,
      call_state_slot = base + 4,
      call_control_slot = base + 5,
      first_result_slot = base + 4,
      last_result_slot = base + 3 + nresults,
    },
    edges = {
      enter_body = { from_pc = prep_pc, to_pc = prep_pc + 1 },
      call_iterator = { from_pc = prep_pc, to_pc = call_pc },
      continue_loop = { from_pc = loop_pc, to_pc = prep_pc + 1 },
      done = { from_pc = loop_pc, to_pc = loop_pc + 1 },
    },
  }
end

function M.find_in_rows(rows)
  local by_pc = row_index(rows)
  local regions, rejects = {}, {}
  for _, row in ipairs(rows or {}) do
    local op = name(row)
    local r = nil
    if op == "FORPREP" then r = numeric_region(row, by_pc, rejects)
    elseif op == "TFORPREP" then r = generic_region(row, by_pc, rejects)
    elseif op == "FORLOOP" then
      -- Matched from FORPREP; standalone FORLOOP is not a scalar region root.
    elseif op == "TFORCALL" or op == "TFORLOOP" then
      -- Matched from TFORPREP; standalone TFOR* members are not roots.
    end
    if r then regions[#regions + 1] = r end
  end
  table.sort(regions, function(a, b)
    if tostring(a.proto) ~= tostring(b.proto) then return tostring(a.proto) < tostring(b.proto) end
    return (a.prep_pc or 0) < (b.prep_pc or 0)
  end)
  return regions, rejects
end

function M.region_key(r)
  if not r then return "" end
  return table.concat({
    tostring(r.dialect or M.DIALECT),
    tostring(r.kind),
    "base=" .. tostring(r.base),
    "prep=" .. tostring(r.prep_pc),
    "body=" .. tostring(r.body_entry_pc),
    "loop=" .. tostring(r.loop_pc),
    "exit=" .. tostring(r.exit_pc),
  }, ":")
end

return M

-- materialize_native_x64.lua -- mechanical copy/link/patch for SpongeJIT x86-64 native stencils.
--
-- This module consumes already-built native stencil descriptors. It never runs
-- SSA, never normalizes, and never infers hidden semantics. Per-site variation
-- enters only through explicit relocation records plus caller-provided concrete
-- bytecode/slot/constant bindings.

local bit = require("bit")

local M = {}

local RELOC_KIND_BY_ID = {
  [0] = "unknown", [1] = "abs32", [2] = "abs32s", [3] = "plt32", [4] = "pc32", [5] = "abs64", [6] = "abs8",
}

local DATA_ROLE_BY_ID = {
  [1] = "slot", [2] = "slot_store", [3] = "imm", [4] = "const", [5] = "bool",
  [6] = "shape_offset", [7] = "shape_id", [8] = "metatable_offset", [9] = "field_offset",
  [10] = "array_base_offset", [11] = "call_target", [12] = "barrier", [13] = "array_len_offset", [14] = "upvalue",
}

local CONTROL_EDGE_BY_ID = {
  [1] = "fallthrough", [2] = "guard_fail", [3] = "boundary", [4] = "projection_stub",
}

local function kind_name(v, by_id)
  if type(v) == "string" then return v end
  return by_id[tonumber(v) or -1] or tostring(v)
end

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function hex_to_array(hex)
  assert(type(hex) == "string" and #hex % 2 == 0, "native stencil code_hex must be an even-length hex string")
  local out = {}
  for pair in hex:gmatch("%x%x") do out[#out + 1] = tonumber(pair, 16) or 0 end
  return out
end

local function array_to_string(bytes)
  local out = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(bytes or {}) do out[i] = string.char(b % 256) end
  return table.concat(out)
end

local function string_to_array(s)
  local out = {}
  for i = 1, #s do out[i] = s:byte(i) end
  return out
end

local function patch_u8(bytes, off, v)
  bytes[off + 1] = (tonumber(v) or 0) % 256
end

local function patch_u32(bytes, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v) or 0
  if n < 0 then n = n + 4294967296 end
  bytes[off + 1] = n % 256
  bytes[off + 2] = math.floor(n / 256) % 256
  bytes[off + 3] = math.floor(n / 65536) % 256
  bytes[off + 4] = math.floor(n / 16777216) % 256
end

local function patch_i64(bytes, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v) or 0
  local neg = n < 0
  if neg then n = -n end
  local b = {}
  for i = 1, 8 do b[i] = n % 256; n = math.floor(n / 256) end
  if neg then
    local carry = 1
    for i = 1, 8 do
      local x = 255 - b[i] + carry
      if x >= 256 then x = x - 256; carry = 1 else carry = 0 end
      b[i] = x
    end
  end
  for i = 1, 8 do bytes[off + i] = b[i] end
end

local function f64_bytes_from_int(value)
  local n = tonumber(value) or 0
  if n == 0 then return {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0} end
  local sign = 0
  if n < 0 then sign = 0x80000000; n = -n end
  local exp = math.floor(math.log(n) / math.log(2))
  local pow = 2 ^ exp
  local mant = math.floor((n / pow - 1) * 2 ^ 52 + 0.5)
  local lo = mant % 4294967296
  local hi = sign + (exp + 1023) * 1048576 + math.floor(mant / 4294967296)
  return {
    lo % 256, math.floor(lo / 256) % 256, math.floor(lo / 65536) % 256, math.floor(lo / 16777216) % 256,
    hi % 256, math.floor(hi / 256) % 256, math.floor(hi / 65536) % 256, math.floor(hi / 16777216) % 256,
  }
end

local function patch_f64_from_int(bytes, off, v)
  local b = f64_bytes_from_int(v)
  for i = 1, 8 do bytes[off + i] = b[i] end
end

local function op_at(entry, op_idx)
  return (entry.ops or entry.source_ops or {})[(tonumber(op_idx) or 0) + 1] or {}
end

local function op_name(op)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tostring(op.op or "") end
  return tostring(op or "")
end

local function logical_slot(entry, role_arg)
  local logical = tonumber(role_arg) or 0
  local slots = entry.actual_slots or entry.slot_bindings or entry.slots or {}
  if slots[logical] ~= nil then return tonumber(slots[logical]) or logical end
  if slots[tostring(logical)] ~= nil then return tonumber(slots[tostring(logical)]) or logical end
  return logical
end

local function default_imm_from_op(op)
  if type(op) ~= "table" then return 0 end
  if op.c ~= nil then return tonumber(op.c) or 0 end
  if op.sC ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sC) or 0 end
  if op.b ~= nil then return tonumber(op.b) or 0 end
  if op.sB ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sB) or 0 end
  if op.sBx ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sBx) or 0 end
  if op.sbx ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sbx) or 0 end
  if op.sJ ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sJ) or 0 end
  if op.sj ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sj) or 0 end
  return 0
end

local function imm_value(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local imms = entry.immediates or {}
  if imms[op_idx] ~= nil then return imms[op_idx] end
  if imms[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return imms[tostring(op_idx)] end
  return default_imm_from_op(op_at(entry, op_idx))
end

local function const_index(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local consts = entry.const_slots or entry.const_indices or {}
  if consts[op_idx] ~= nil then return tonumber(consts[op_idx]) or 0 end
  if consts[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return tonumber(consts[tostring(op_idx)]) or 0 end
  local op = op_at(entry, op_idx)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tonumber(op.bx or op.ax or op.b or op.c or op.const or 0) or 0 end
  return 0
end

local function const_i64_value(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local vals = entry.const_i64s or entry.const_values or entry.k_i64s or entry.constants or {}
  if vals[op_idx] ~= nil then return tonumber(vals[op_idx]) or 0 end
  if vals[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return tonumber(vals[tostring(op_idx)]) or 0 end
  local op = op_at(entry, op_idx)
  if type(op) == "table" then
    if op.k_i64 ~= nil then return tonumber(op.k_i64) or 0 end
    if op.const_i64 ~= nil then return tonumber(op.const_i64) or 0 end
  end
  error("missing i64 constant value for native const patch at op_idx " .. tostring(op_idx))
end

local function bool_tag(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local bools = entry.booleans or {}
  local v = bools[op_idx]
  if v == nil then v = bools[tostring(op_idx)] end
  if v == nil then
    local name = op_name(op_at(entry, op_idx))
    if name == "LOADFALSE" or name == "LFALSESKIP" then v = false else v = true end
  end
  return v and 17 or 1 -- PUC Lua 5.5 LUA_VTRUE / LUA_VFALSE tags.
end

local function payload_value(entry, r, plural, singular)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local vals = entry[plural] or {}
  if vals[op_idx] ~= nil then return tonumber(vals[op_idx]) or 0 end
  if vals[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return tonumber(vals[tostring(op_idx)]) or 0 end
  if entry[singular] ~= nil then return tonumber(entry[singular]) or 0 end
  local payloads = entry.payloads or {}
  if payloads[singular] ~= nil then return tonumber(payloads[singular]) or 0 end
  if payloads[plural] and payloads[plural][op_idx] ~= nil then return tonumber(payloads[plural][op_idx]) or 0 end
  if payloads[plural] and payloads[plural][tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return tonumber(payloads[plural][tostring(op_idx)]) or 0 end
  local op = op_at(entry, op_idx)
  if type(op) == "table" and op[singular] ~= nil then return tonumber(op[singular]) or 0 end
  error("missing native payload value for " .. tostring(singular) .. " at op_idx " .. tostring(op_idx))
end

local function data_patch_value(entry, r)
  local role = kind_name(r.role_kind, DATA_ROLE_BY_ID)
  local addend = tonumber(r.addend) or 0
  if role == "slot" or role == "slot_store" then
    return logical_slot(entry, r.role_arg) * 16 + addend
  elseif role == "imm" then
    return imm_value(entry, r) + addend
  elseif role == "const" then
    return const_index(entry, r) * 16 + addend
  elseif role == "bool" then
    return bool_tag(entry, r) + addend
  elseif role == "shape_offset" then
    return payload_value(entry, r, "shape_offsets", "shape_offset") + addend
  elseif role == "shape_id" then
    return payload_value(entry, r, "shape_ids", "shape_id") + addend
  elseif role == "metatable_offset" then
    return payload_value(entry, r, "metatable_offsets", "metatable_offset") + addend
  elseif role == "field_offset" then
    return payload_value(entry, r, "field_offsets", "field_offset") + addend
  elseif role == "array_base_offset" then
    return payload_value(entry, r, "array_base_offsets", "array_base_offset") + addend
  elseif role == "array_len_offset" then
    return payload_value(entry, r, "array_len_offsets", "array_len_offset") + addend
  elseif role == "upvalue" then
    local op = op_at(entry, tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0)
    local idx = 0
    if type(op) == "table" then
      local name = op_name(op)
      if name == "SETTABUP" then idx = tonumber(op.a) or 0
      elseif name == "GETTABUP" or name == "GETUPVAL" then idx = tonumber(op.b) or 0
      elseif name == "SETUPVAL" then idx = tonumber(op.b) or 0
      else idx = tonumber(op.b or op.a or 0) or 0 end
    end
    local upvals = entry.upvalues or entry.upvalue_indices or {}
    if upvals[tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0] ~= nil then idx = tonumber(upvals[tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0]) or idx end
    if upvals[tostring(tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0)] ~= nil then idx = tonumber(upvals[tostring(tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0)]) or idx end
    return idx * 8 + addend
  end
  error("unsupported materializer data role: " .. tostring(role))
end

local function patch_data_reloc(bytes, base, entry, r)
  local reloc = kind_name(r.reloc_kind, RELOC_KIND_BY_ID)
  local off = base + (tonumber(r.code_offset) or 0)
  local op = op_at(entry, tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0)
  local role = kind_name(r.role_kind, DATA_ROLE_BY_ID)
  local v = data_patch_value(entry, r)
  if reloc == "abs8" then patch_u8(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs32" or reloc == "abs32s" then patch_u32(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs64" and role == "imm" and op_name(op) == "LOADF" then patch_f64_from_int(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs64" and role == "const" then patch_i64(bytes, off, const_i64_value(entry, r))
  elseif reloc == "abs64" then patch_i64(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "unknown" then error("native data reloc has unknown encoding at offset " .. tostring(off))
  else error("unsupported materializer data reloc encoding: " .. tostring(reloc)) end
end

local function normalize_entry(e)
  if e.stencil then return e end
  return { stencil = e }
end

function M.materialize(entries, opts)
  opts = opts or {}
  assert(type(entries) == "table" and #entries > 0, "materialize requires at least one stencil entry")
  local norm = {}
  local image = {}
  local offset = 0

  for i, raw in ipairs(entries) do
    local entry = normalize_entry(raw)
    local st = assert(entry.stencil, "entry missing stencil")
    if st.executable ~= true then error("cannot materialize non-executable stencil at entry " .. tostring(i)) end
    local flags = tonumber(st.abi and st.abi.stencil and st.abi.stencil.flags or st.flags or 0) or 0
    if st.exact == false or (st.exact ~= true and bit.band(flags, 8) == 0) then
      error("cannot materialize non-exact stencil at entry " .. tostring(i))
    end
    if type(st.code_hex) ~= "string" or #st.code_hex == 0 then error("native stencil missing code_hex at entry " .. tostring(i)) end
    local bytes = hex_to_array(st.code_hex)
    norm[i] = {
      stencil = st,
      ops = entry.ops or st.source_ops or {},
      source_ops = entry.source_ops or st.source_ops or {},
      actual_slots = entry.actual_slots or entry.slot_bindings or entry.slots,
      slot_bindings = entry.slot_bindings,
      immediates = entry.immediates,
      const_slots = entry.const_slots,
      const_indices = entry.const_indices,
      const_i64s = entry.const_i64s,
      const_values = entry.const_values,
      k_i64s = entry.k_i64s,
      constants = entry.constants,
      booleans = entry.booleans,
      payloads = entry.payloads,
      shape_offsets = entry.shape_offsets,
      shape_offset = entry.shape_offset,
      shape_ids = entry.shape_ids,
      shape_id = entry.shape_id,
      metatable_offsets = entry.metatable_offsets,
      metatable_offset = entry.metatable_offset,
      field_offsets = entry.field_offsets,
      field_offset = entry.field_offset,
      array_base_offsets = entry.array_base_offsets,
      array_base_offset = entry.array_base_offset,
      array_len_offsets = entry.array_len_offsets,
      array_len_offset = entry.array_len_offset,
      upvalues = entry.upvalues,
      upvalue_indices = entry.upvalue_indices,
      base = offset,
      size = #bytes,
    }
    for _, b in ipairs(bytes) do image[#image + 1] = b end
    offset = offset + #bytes
  end

  for _, entry in ipairs(norm) do
    for _, r in ipairs(entry.stencil.data_relocs or {}) do patch_data_reloc(image, entry.base, entry, r) end
  end

  for i, entry in ipairs(norm) do
    for _, r in ipairs(entry.stencil.control_relocs or {}) do
      local edge = kind_name(r.edge_kind, CONTROL_EDGE_BY_ID)
      local reloc = kind_name(r.reloc_kind, RELOC_KIND_BY_ID)
      if edge == "fallthrough" and i < #norm then
        if reloc ~= "pc32" then error("fallthrough control reloc must be pc32") end
        local site = entry.base + (tonumber(r.code_offset) or 0)
        local target = norm[i + 1].base
        patch_u32(image, site, target - (site + 4))
      elseif edge ~= "fallthrough" and edge ~= "guard_fail" and edge ~= "boundary" and edge ~= "projection_stub" then
        error("unknown control edge kind: " .. tostring(edge))
      end
    end
  end

  local bytes = array_to_string(image)
  return {
    ok = true,
    bytes = bytes,
    code_size = #bytes,
    entries = norm,
  }
end

function M.alloc_executable(bytes)
  local ok, ffi = pcall(require, "ffi")
  if not ok then return nil, "ffi unavailable" end
  ffi.cdef[[
    typedef unsigned long size_t;
    void *mmap(void *addr, size_t length, int prot, int flags, int fd, long offset);
    int munmap(void *addr, size_t length);
    void *memcpy(void *dest, const void *src, size_t n);
  ]]
  local PROT_READ, PROT_WRITE, PROT_EXEC = 1, 2, 4
  local MAP_PRIVATE, MAP_ANON = 2, 0x20
  local n = #bytes
  local mem = ffi.C.mmap(nil, n, PROT_READ + PROT_WRITE + PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE + MAP_ANON, -1, 0)
  if mem == ffi.cast("void *", -1) then return nil, "mmap failed" end
  local src = ffi.new("uint8_t[?]", n)
  local arr = string_to_array(bytes)
  for i, b in ipairs(arr) do src[i - 1] = b end
  ffi.C.memcpy(mem, src, n)
  return mem, n
end

function M.free_executable(mem, n)
  local ok, ffi = pcall(require, "ffi")
  if not ok then return nil, "ffi unavailable" end
